dtcs.description: Cushion Peltier Temperature Sensor Malfunction Outputs from the seat climate control controller stop if one of the following occurs: 1) the seat climate control controller's temperature sensor is open or shorted; or 2) the temperature sensor is damaged and its output value does not change. PROCEDURE

steps.instruction: CHECK FOR DTC (a) Check for DTCs    See: Body and Frame/Seats/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes/Climate Control Seat System. Result B --  READ VALUE USING TECHSTREAM (SEAT CUSHION TEMPERATURE SENSOR) C --  READ VALUE USING TECHSTREAM (SEAT CUSHION TEMPERATURE SENSOR) D --  READ VALUE USING TECHSTREAM (SEAT CUSHION TEMPERATURE SENSOR) A -- Continue to next step.

READ VALUE USING TECHSTREAM (SEAT CUSHION TEMPERATURE SENSOR) (a) Check the Data List for proper functioning of the temperature sensor. Front Left Seat A/C OK: The display is as specified in the normal condition. NG --  INSPECT SEAT CLIMATE CONTROL CONTROLLER LH OK --  REPLACE SEAT CLIMATE CONTROL ECU  See: Body and Frame/Seats/Service and Repair/Overhaul/Front Seat Assembly/Disassembly

INSPECT SEAT CLIMATE CONTROL CONTROLLER LH (a) Remove the e33 climate control controller connector. (b) Measure the resistance according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard resistance: NG --  REPLACE SEAT CLIMATE CONTROL CONTROLLER LH  See: Body and Frame/Seats/Service and Repair/Overhaul/Front Seat Assembly/Disassembly OK -- Continue to next step.

CHECK HARNESS AND CONNECTOR (SEAT CLIMATE CONTROL ECU - SEAT CLIMATE CONTROL CONTROLLER LH AND BODY GROUND) (a) Disconnect the e34 ECU connector. (b) Disconnect the e33 climate control controller connector. (c) Measure the resistance according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard resistance: NG --  REPAIR OR REPLACE HARNESS OR CONNECTOR OK --  REPLACE SEAT CLIMATE CONTROL ECU  See: Body and Frame/Seats/Service and Repair/Overhaul/Front Seat Assembly/Disassembly

READ VALUE USING TECHSTREAM (SEAT CUSHION TEMPERATURE SENSOR) (a) Check the Data List for proper functioning of the temperature sensor. Front Right Seat A/C OK: The display is as specified in the normal condition. NG --  INSPECT SEAT CLIMATE CONTROL CONTROLLER RH OK --  REPLACE SEAT CLIMATE CONTROL ECU  See: Body and Frame/Seats/Service and Repair/Overhaul/Front Seat Assembly/Disassembly

INSPECT SEAT CLIMATE CONTROL CONTROLLER RH (a) Remove the e2 climate control controller connector. (b) Measure the resistance according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard resistance: NG --  REPLACE SEAT CLIMATE CONTROL CONTROLLER RH  See: Body and Frame/Seats/Service and Repair/Overhaul/Front Seat Assembly/Disassembly OK -- Continue to next step.

CHECK HARNESS AND CONNECTOR (SEAT CLIMATE CONTROL ECU - SEAT CLIMATE CONTROL CONTROLLER RH AND BODY GROUND) (a) Disconnect the e4 ECU connector. (b) Disconnect the e2 climate control controller connector. (c) Measure the resistance according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard resistance: NG --  REPAIR OR REPLACE HARNESS OR CONNECTOR OK --  REPLACE SEAT CLIMATE CONTROL ECU  See: Body and Frame/Seats/Service and Repair/Overhaul/Front Seat Assembly/Disassembly

READ VALUE USING TECHSTREAM (SEAT CUSHION TEMPERATURE SENSOR) (a) Check the Data List for proper functioning of the temperature sensor. Rear Left Seat A/C OK: The display is as specified in the normal condition. Result B --  REPLACE SEAT CLIMATE CONTROL ECU  See: Body and Frame/Seats/Service and Repair/Overhaul/Rear Seat Assembly (for Ottoman)/Disassembly C --  INSPECT REAR SEAT CLIMATE CONTROL CONTROLLER LH A --  REPLACE SEAT CLIMATE CONTROL ECU  See: Body and Frame/Seats/Service and Repair/Overhaul/Rear Seat Assembly (for Power Seat)/Disassembly

INSPECT REAR SEAT CLIMATE CONTROL CONTROLLER LH (a) Remove the p3 climate control controller connector. (b) Measure the resistance according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard resistance: Result B --  REPLACE REAR SEAT CLIMATE CONTROL CONTROLLER LH  See: Body and Frame/Seats/Service and Repair/Overhaul/Rear Seat Assembly (for Power Seat)/Disassembly C --  REPLACE REAR SEAT CLIMATE CONTROL CONTROLLER LH  See: Body and Frame/Seats/Service and Repair/Overhaul/Rear Seat Assembly (for Ottoman)/Disassembly A -- Continue to next step.

CHECK HARNESS AND CONNECTOR (HEATER BLOWER WIRE) (a) Disconnect the fp2 joining connector. (b) Disconnect the p3 climate control controller connector. (c) Measure the resistance according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard resistance: NG --  REPAIR OR REPLACE HARNESS OR CONNECTOR (HEATER BLOWER WIRE) OK --  REPAIR OR REPLACE HARNESS OR CONNECTOR (SEAT CLIMATE CONTROL ECU - JOINING CONNECTOR)

READ VALUE USING TECHSTREAM (SEAT CUSHION TEMPERATURE SENSOR) (a) Check the Data List for proper functioning of the temperature sensor. Rear Right Seat A/C OK: The display is as specified in the normal condition. Result B --  REPLACE SEAT CLIMATE CONTROL ECU  See: Body and Frame/Seats/Service and Repair/Overhaul/Rear Seat Assembly (for Power Seat)/Disassembly C --  REPLACE SEAT CLIMATE CONTROL ECU  See: Body and Frame/Seats/Service and Repair/Overhaul/Rear Seat Assembly (for Ottoman)/Disassembly A -- Continue to next step.

INSPECT REAR SEAT CLIMATE CONTROL CONTROLLER RH (a) Remove the p1 climate control controller connector. (b) Measure the resistance according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard resistance: Result B --  REPLACE REAR SEAT CLIMATE CONTROL CONTROLLER RH  See: Body and Frame/Seats/Service and Repair/Overhaul/Rear Seat Assembly (for Power Seat)/Disassembly C --  REPLACE REAR SEAT CLIMATE CONTROL CONTROLLER RH  See: Body and Frame/Seats/Service and Repair/Overhaul/Rear Seat Assembly (for Ottoman)/Disassembly A -- Continue to next step.

CHECK HARNESS AND CONNECTOR (HEATER BLOWER WIRE) (a) Disconnect the fp1 joining connector. (b) Disconnect the p1 climate control controller connector. (c) Measure the resistance according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard resistance: NG --  REPAIR OR REPLACE HARNESS OR CONNECTOR (HEATER BLOWER WIRE) OK --  REPAIR OR REPLACE HARNESS OR CONNECTOR (SEAT CLIMATE CONTROL ECU - JOINING CONNECTOR)

dtcs.description: Seatback Peltier Temperature Sensor Malfunction Outputs to the seat climate control controller or rear seatback climate controller blower stop if one of the following occurs: 1) the seat climate control controller temperature sensor or rear seatback climate control blower temperature sensor is open or shorted; 2) either temperature sensor is damaged and its output value does not change. PROCEDURE

steps.instruction: CHECK FOR DTC (a) Check for DTCs    See: Body and Frame/Seats/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes/Climate Control Seat System. Result B --  READ VALUE USING TECHSTREAM (SEATBACK TEMPERATURE SENSOR) C --  READ VALUE USING TECHSTREAM (SEATBACK TEMPERATURE SENSOR) D --  READ VALUE USING TECHSTREAM (SEATBACK TEMPERATURE SENSOR) A -- Continue to next step.

READ VALUE USING TECHSTREAM (SEATBACK TEMPERATURE SENSOR) (a) Check the Data List for proper functioning of the temperature sensor. Front Left Seat A/C OK: The display is as specified in the normal condition. NG --  INSPECT SEAT CLIMATE CONTROL CONTROLLER LH OK --  REPLACE SEAT CLIMATE CONTROL ECU  See: Body and Frame/Seats/Service and Repair/Overhaul/Front Seat Assembly/Disassembly

INSPECT SEAT CLIMATE CONTROL CONTROLLER LH (a) Remove the e51 climate control controller connector. (b) Measure the resistance according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard resistance: NG --  REPLACE SEAT CLIMATE CONTROL CONTROLLER LH  See: Body and Frame/Seats/Service and Repair/Overhaul/Front Seat Assembly/Disassembly OK -- Continue to next step.

CHECK HARNESS AND CONNECTOR (SEAT CLIMATE CONTROL ECU - SEAT CLIMATE CONTROL CONTROLLER LH AND BODY GROUND) (a) Disconnect the e34 ECU connector. (b) Disconnect the e51 climate control controller connector. (c) Measure the resistance according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard resistance: NG --  REPAIR OR REPLACE HARNESS OR CONNECTOR OK --  REPLACE SEAT CLIMATE CONTROL ECU  See: Body and Frame/Seats/Service and Repair/Overhaul/Front Seat Assembly/Disassembly

READ VALUE USING TECHSTREAM (SEATBACK TEMPERATURE SENSOR) (a) Check the Data List for proper functioning of the temperature sensor. Front Right Seat A/C OK: The display is as specified in the normal condition. NG --  INSPECT SEAT CLIMATE CONTROL CONTROLLER RH OK --  REPLACE SEAT CLIMATE CONTROL ECU  See: Body and Frame/Seats/Service and Repair/Overhaul/Front Seat Assembly/Disassembly

INSPECT SEAT CLIMATE CONTROL CONTROLLER RH (a) Remove the e21 climate control controller connector. (b) Measure the resistance according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard resistance: NG --  REPLACE SEAT CLIMATE CONTROL CONTROLLER RH  See: Body and Frame/Seats/Service and Repair/Overhaul/Front Seat Assembly/Disassembly OK -- Continue to next step.

CHECK HARNESS AND CONNECTOR (SEAT CLIMATE CONTROL ECU - SEAT CLIMATE CONTROL CONTROLLER RH AND BODY GROUND) (a) Disconnect the e4 ECU connector. (b) Disconnect the e21 climate control controller connector. (c) Measure the resistance according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard resistance: NG --  REPAIR OR REPLACE HARNESS OR CONNECTOR OK --  REPLACE SEAT CLIMATE CONTROL ECU  See: Body and Frame/Seats/Service and Repair/Overhaul/Front Seat Assembly/Disassembly

READ VALUE USING TECHSTREAM (SEATBACK TEMPERATURE SENSOR) (a) Check the Data List for proper functioning of the temperature sensor. Rear Left Seat A/C OK: The display is as specified in the normal condition. Result B --  REPLACE SEAT CLIMATE CONTROL ECU  See: Body and Frame/Seats/Service and Repair/Overhaul/Rear Seat Assembly (for Ottoman)/Disassembly C --  INSPECT REAR SEATBACK CLIMATE CONTROL BLOWER LH A --  REPLACE SEAT CLIMATE CONTROL ECU  See: Body and Frame/Seats/Service and Repair/Overhaul/Rear Seat Assembly (for Power Seat)/Disassembly

INSPECT REAR SEATBACK CLIMATE CONTROL BLOWER LH (a) Remove the f32 climate control blower connector. (b) Measure the resistance according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard resistance: Result B --  REPLACE REAR SEATBACK CLIMATE CONTROL BLOWER LH  See: Body and Frame/Seats/Service and Repair/Overhaul/Rear Seat Assembly (for Power Seat)/Disassembly C --  REPLACE REAR SEAT BACK CLIMATE CONTROL BLOWER LH  See: Body and Frame/Seats/Service and Repair/Overhaul/Rear Seat Assembly (for Ottoman)/Disassembly A -- Continue to next step.

READ VALUE USING TECHSTREAM (SEATBACK TEMPERATURE SENSOR) (a) Check the Data List for proper functioning of the temperature sensor. Rear Right Seat A/C OK: The display is as specified in the normal condition. NG --  INSPECT REAR SEAT CUSHION COVER LH OK --  REPLACE SEAT CLIMATE CONTROL ECU LH  See: Body and Frame/Seats/Service and Repair/Overhaul/Rear Seat Assembly (for Fixed Seat Type)/Disassembly

dtcs.description: Overcurrent Detected in Cushion Peltier The seat climate control ECU stops outputs to the seat climate control controller if a short, etc. in the seat climate control controller, wire harness or ECU interior circuit causes overcurrent. PROCEDURE

steps.instruction: CHECK FOR DTC (a) Check for DTCs    See: Body and Frame/Seats/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes/Climate Control Seat System. Result B --  CHECK HARNESS AND CONNECTOR (SEAT CLIMATE CONTROL ECU - SEAT CLIMATE CONTROL CONTROLLER RH) C --  CHECK HARNESS AND CONNECTOR (SEAT CLIMATE CONTROL ECU - JOINING CONNECTOR AND REAR SEATBACK CLIMATE CONTROL BLOWER LH) D --  CHECK HARNESS AND CONNECTOR (SEAT CLIMATE CONTROL ECU - JOINING CONNECTOR AND REAR SEATBACK CLIMATE CONTROL BLOWER RH) A -- Continue to next step.

CHECK HARNESS AND CONNECTOR (SEAT CLIMATE CONTROL ECU - SEAT CLIMATE CONTROL CONTROLLER LH) (a) Disconnect the e34 ECU connector. (b) Disconnect the e33 and e51 climate control controller connectors. (c) Measure the resistance according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard resistance: NG --  REPAIR OR REPLACE HARNESS OR CONNECTOR OK -- Continue to next step.

CHECK SEAT CLIMATE CONTROL CONTROLLER LH (for SEAT CUSHION) (a) Replace the seat climate control controller LH (for Seat Cushion)    See: Body and Frame/Seats/Service and Repair/Overhaul/Front Seat Assembly/Disassembly. (b) Check for DTC    See: Body and Frame/Seats/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes/Climate Control Seat System. OK: DTC is not output NG --  CHECK SEAT CLIMATE CONTROL CONTROLLER LH (for SEATBACK) OK --  END (SEAT CLIMATE CONTROL CONTROLLER LH [for SEAT CUSHION] WAS DEFECTIVE)

CHECK SEAT CLIMATE CONTROL CONTROLLER LH (for SEATBACK) (a) Replace the seat climate control controller LH (for Seatback)    See: Body and Frame/Seats/Service and Repair/Overhaul/Front Seat Assembly/Disassembly. (b) Check for DTC    See: Body and Frame/Seats/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes/Climate Control Seat System. OK: DTC is not output NG --  REPLACE SEAT CLIMATE CONTROL ECU  See: Body and Frame/Seats/Service and Repair/Overhaul/Front Seat Assembly/Disassembly OK --  END (SEAT CLIMATE CONTROL CONTROLLER LH [for SEATBACK] WAS DEFECTIVE)

CHECK HARNESS AND CONNECTOR (SEAT CLIMATE CONTROL ECU - SEAT CLIMATE CONTROL CONTROLLER RH) (a) Disconnect the e4 ECU connector. (b) Disconnect the e2 and e21 climate control controller connectors. (c) Measure the resistance according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard resistance: NG --  REPAIR OR REPLACE HARNESS OR CONNECTOR OK -- Continue to next step.

CHECK SEAT CLIMATE CONTROL CONTROLLER RH (for SEAT CUSHION) (a) Replace the seat climate control controller RH    See: Body and Frame/Seats/Service and Repair/Overhaul/Front Seat Assembly/Disassembly. (b) Check for DTC    See: Body and Frame/Seats/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes/Climate Control Seat System. OK: DTC is not output NG --  CHECK SEAT CLIMATE CONTROL CONTROLLER RH (for SEATBACK) OK --  END (SEAT CLIMATE CONTROL CONTROLLER RH [for SEAT CUSHION] WAS DEFECTIVE)

CHECK SEAT CLIMATE CONTROL CONTROLLER RH (for SEATBACK) (a) Replace the seat climate control controller RH    See: Body and Frame/Seats/Service and Repair/Overhaul/Front Seat Assembly/Disassembly. (b) Check for DTC    See: Body and Frame/Seats/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes/Climate Control Seat System. OK: DTC is not output NG --  REPLACE SEAT CLIMATE CONTROL ECU  See: Body and Frame/Seats/Service and Repair/Overhaul/Front Seat Assembly/Disassembly OK --  END (SEAT CLIMATE CONTROL CONTROLLER RH [for SEATBACK] WAS DEFECTIVE)

CHECK HARNESS AND CONNECTOR (SEAT CLIMATE CONTROL ECU - JOINING CONNECTOR AND REAR SEATBACK CLIMATE CONTROL BLOWER LH) (a) Disconnect the f28 ECU connector. (b) Disconnect the fp2 joining connector. (c) Disconnect the f32 rear seatback climate control blower connector. (d) Measure the resistance according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard resistance: NG --  REPAIR OR REPLACE HARNESS OR CONNECTOR OK -- Continue to next step.

CHECK HARNESS AND CONNECTOR (JOINING CONNECTOR - SEAT CLIMATE CONTROL CONTROLLER LH) (a) Disconnect the fp2 joining connector. (b) Disconnect the p3 climate control controller connector. (c) Measure the resistance according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard resistance: NG --  REPAIR OR REPLACE HARNESS OR CONNECTOR OK -- Continue to next step.

CHECK SEAT CLIMATE CONTROL CONTROLLER LH (a) w/o Ottoman: Replace the seat climate control controller LH    See: Body and Frame/Seats/Service and Repair/Overhaul/Rear Seat Assembly (for Power Seat)/Disassembly. (b) w/ Ottoman: Replace the seat climate control controller LH    See: Body and Frame/Seats/Service and Repair/Overhaul/Rear Seat Assembly (for Ottoman)/Disassembly. (c) Check for DTC    See: Body and Frame/Seats/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes/Climate Control Seat System. OK: DTC is not output NG --  CHECK REAR SEAT BACK CLIMATE CONTROL BLOWER LH OK --  END (SEAT CLIMATE CONTROL CONTROLLER LH WAS DEFECTIVE)

CHECK REAR SEAT BACK CLIMATE CONTROL BLOWER LH (a) w/o Ottoman: Replace the rear seatback climate control blower LH    See: Body and Frame/Seats/Service and Repair/Overhaul/Rear Seat Assembly (for Power Seat)/Disassembly. (b) w/ Ottoman: Replace the rear seatback climate control blower LH    See: Body and Frame/Seats/Service and Repair/Overhaul/Rear Seat Assembly (for Ottoman)/Disassembly. (c) Check for DTC    See: Body and Frame/Seats/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes/Climate Control Seat System. OK: DTC is not output Result B --  REPLACE SEAT CLIMATE CONTROL ECU  See: Body and Frame/Seats/Service and Repair/Overhaul/Rear Seat Assembly (for Ottoman)/Disassembly C --  END (REAR SEATBACK CLIMATE CONTROL BLOWER LH WAS DEFECTIVE) A --  REPLACE SEAT CLIMATE CONTROL ECU  See: Body and Frame/Seats/Service and Repair/Overhaul/Rear Seat Assembly (for Power Seat)/Disassembly

CHECK HARNESS AND CONNECTOR (SEAT CLIMATE CONTROL ECU - JOINING CONNECTOR AND REAR SEATBACK CLIMATE CONTROL BLOWER RH) (a) Disconnect the f8 ECU connector. (b) Disconnect the fp1 joining connector. (c) Disconnect the f12 rear seatback climate control blower connector. (d) Measure the resistance according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard resistance: NG --  REPAIR OR REPLACE HARNESS OR CONNECTOR OK -- Continue to next step.

CHECK HARNESS AND CONNECTOR (JOINING CONNECTOR - SEAT CLIMATE CONTROL CONTROLLER RH) (a) Disconnect the fp1 joining connector. (b) Disconnect the p1 climate control controller connector. (c) Measure the resistance according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard resistance: NG --  REPAIR OR REPLACE HARNESS OR CONNECTOR OK -- Continue to next step.

CHECK SEAT CLIMATE CONTROL CONTROLLER RH (a) w/o Ottoman: Replace the seat climate control controller RH    See: Body and Frame/Seats/Service and Repair/Overhaul/Rear Seat Assembly (for Power Seat)/Disassembly. (b) w/ Ottoman: Replace the seat climate control controller RH    See: Body and Frame/Seats/Service and Repair/Overhaul/Rear Seat Assembly (for Ottoman)/Disassembly. dtcs.description: Telematics Transceiver Malfunction This DTC is stored when an error in the EEPROM or PLL IC is detected during the DCM (telematics transceiver) self-check. The EEPROM (Electrically Erasable Programmable Read-Only Memory) stores the various data to operate the Safety Connect System. The PLL IC (Phase-Locked Loop Integrated Circuit) oscillates the telephone radio frequency. NOTICE: Regardless of whether DTC B15A8 has been output as a present or past DTC, the DCM (telematics transceiver) should be replaced. PROCEDURE

steps.instruction: REPLACE DCM (TELEMATICS TRANSCEIVER) (a) Replace the DCM (telematics transceiver)    See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Emergency Contact Module/Service and Repair/Removal. NOTICE: * The engine switch must be off. * Do not replace the DCM (telematics transceiver) with one from another vehicle. NEXT --  PERFORM DCM ACTIVATION  See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Emergency Contact Module/Testing and Inspection/Scan Tool Testing and Procedures/DCM Activation Audio and Visual System (w/ Multi-Display)

dtcs.description: Multi-media Interface ECU Malfunction This DTC is stored when a malfunction occurs in the multi-media interface ECU. HINT After the inspection is completed, clear the DTC. PROCEDURE

steps.instruction: REPLACE "iPod" OR USB DEVICE (a) Disconnect the USB device or "iPod" from the No. 1 stereo jack adapter assembly. (b) Turn the engine switch off. HINT When this DTC has been stored, it is necessary to turn off the engine switch to make it possible for the vehicle to recognize a new device when it is connected. (c) Turn the engine switch on (ACC). (d) Connect a known good USB device or "iPod" to the No. 1 stereo jack adapter assembly. HINT If the malfunction occurred when a USB device was in use, use another USB device for the inspection. If the malfunction occurred when an "iPod" was in use, use another "iPod" for the inspection. NEXT -- Continue to next step.

CHECK FOR DTC (a) Clear the DTCs    See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Radio, Stereo, and Compact Disc/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes/Audio and Visual System (w/ Multi-Display). dtcs.description: Speed Signal Malfunction The display and navigation module display receives a vehicle speed signal from the No. 1 meter ECU sub-assembly and information about the navigation antenna, and then adjusts vehicle position. The display and navigation module display stores this DTC when the difference between the speed information that the navigation antenna receives and the SPD pulse received from the No. 1 meter ECU sub-assembly becomes large. HINT * A voltage of 12 V or 5 V is output from each ECU and then input to the No. 1 meter ECU sub-assembly. The signal is changed to a pulse signal at the transistor in the No. 1 meter ECU sub-assembly. Each ECU controls the respective systems based on the pulse signal. * If a short occurs in any of the ECUs or in the wire harness connected to an ECU, all systems in the diagram below will not operate normally. PROCEDURE

steps.instruction: CHECK NO. 1 METER ECU SUB-ASSEMBLY (a) Drive the vehicle and check if the function of the speedometer in the No.1 meter ECU sub-assembly is normal. OK: Actual vehicle speed and the speed indicated on the speedometer are the same. HINT The vehicle speed sensor is functioning normally when the indication on the speedometer is normal. NG --  GO TO METER / GAUGE SYSTEM  See: Instrument Panel, Gauges and Warning Indicators/Testing and Inspection/Initial Inspection and Diagnostic Overview/How To Proceed With Troubleshooting OK -- Continue to next step.

CHECK VEHICLE SENSOR (OPERATION CHECK) (a) Enter the "GPS & Vehicle Sensor" (Vehicle Sensors)    See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Navigation System/Testing and Inspection/Initial Inspection and Diagnostic Overview/Navigation System (for DVD)/Operation Check. (b) While driving the vehicle, compare the "SPD" indicator to the reading on the speedometer. Check if these readings are equal or almost equal. OK: The readings are equal or almost equal. NG --  CHECK NO. 1 METER ECU SUB-ASSEMBLY (SPEED SIGNAL) OK -- Continue to next step.

CHECK HARNESS AND CONNECTOR (NAVIGATION RECEIVER - NO. 1 METER ECU) (a) Disconnect the L59 display and navigation module display connector. (b) Disconnect the L10 No. 1 meter ECU sub-assembly connector. (c) Measure the resistance according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard Resistance: NG --  REPAIR OR REPLACE HARNESS OR CONNECTOR OK -- Continue to next step.

CHECK NO. 1 METER ECU SUB-ASSEMBLY (SPEED SIGNAL) (a) Remove the No. 1 meter ECU sub-assembly with the connector(s) still connected    See: Instrument Panel, Gauges and Warning Indicators/Instrument Cluster / Carrier/Service and Repair/Removal and Replacement/Removal. (b) Connect an oscilloscope to terminal L10-10 (+S) and body ground. (c) Turn the engine switch on (IG). (d) Check the signal waveform according to the condition(s) in the table below. Measurement Condition OK: The waveform display is as shown in the illustration. HINT When the system is function normally, one wheel revolution generates 4 pulses. As the vehicle speed increases, the width indicated by (A) in the illustration narrows. Text in Illustration NG --  REPLACE NO. 1 METER ECU SUB-ASSEMBLY  See: Instrument Panel, Gauges and Warning Indicators/Instrument Cluster / Carrier/Service and Repair/Removal and Replacement/Removal OK --  REPLACE DISPLAY AND NAVIGATION MODULE DISPLAY  See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Navigation System/Navigation Display/Service and Repair/Removal and Replacement/Multi-Display (w/ Navigation System)/Removal Navigation System (for HDD)

(c) Turn the engine switch on (IG) and wait for 10 seconds. (d) Enter the following menus: Body Electrical / Telematics / Data List. (e) Check that the manual (SOS) switch condition displayed on the Techstream changes according to the manual (SOS) switch operation. dtcs.description: Rear Console Recognition Disconnected The display and navigation module display and rear power seat switch are connected by the AVC-LAN communication line. When an AVC-LAN communication error occurs between the display and navigation display and rear power seat switch, this DTC will be stored. HINT * Even if no fault is present, this DTC may be stored depending on the battery condition or engine start voltage. * The display and navigation module display is the master unit. NOTICE: Inspect the fuses for circuits related to this system before performing the following inspection procedure. PROCEDURE

steps.instruction: CHECK OPTIONAL COMPONENTS (INCLUDING ASSOCIATED WIRING) (a) Check that optional components (including associated wiring) which generate radio waves are not installed. Result HINT * Electrical noise from radio waves generated by optional components or the wiring for those components may affect AVC-LAN communication. * This DTC may be stored when an AVC-LAN communication error occurs due to electrical noise. B --  CHECK FOR DTC A -- Continue to next step.

REMOVE OPTIONAL COMPONENTS (a) Remove optional components (including associated wiring). NOTICE: Do not remove optional components or associated wiring without the permission of the customer. NEXT -- Continue to next step.

CHECK FOR DTC (a) Clear the DTCs    See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Accessory Control Display/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes. (b) Check for DTCs and check if the same trouble occurs again. OK: No DTCs are output. NG --  CHECK HARNESS AND CONNECTOR (REAR POWER SEAT SWITCH - BATTERY AND BODY GROUND) OK --  END

CHECK HARNESS AND CONNECTOR (REAR POWER SEAT SWITCH - BATTERY AND BODY GROUND) (a) Disconnect the S15*1 or r2*2 rear power seat switch connector. *1: for 4-Passenger with Ottoman *2: except 4-Passenger with Ottoman (b) Measure the resistance according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard Resistance: for 4-Passenger with Ottoman except 4-Passenger with Ottoman (c) Measure the voltage according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard Voltage: for 4-Passenger with Ottoman except 4-Passenger with Ottoman Text in Illustration NG --  REPAIR OR REPLACE HARNESS OR CONNECTOR OK -- Continue to next step.

CHECK HARNESS AND CONNECTOR (REAR POWER SEAT SWITCH - MULTI-MEDIA INTERFACE ECU) (a) Disconnect the S15*1 or r2*2 rear power seat switch connector. *1: for 4-Passenger with Ottoman *2: except 4-Passenger with Ottoman (b) Disconnect the R66 multi-media interface ECU. (c) Measure the resistance according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard Resistance: for 4-Passenger with Ottoman except 4-Passenger with Ottoman NG --  REPAIR OR REPLACE HARNESS OR CONNECTOR OK -- Continue to next step.

REPLACE REAR POWER SEAT SWITCH (a) Temporarily replace the rear power seat switch with a new or normally functioning one. (b) Clear the DTCs    See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Accessory Control Display/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes. (c) Check for DTCs and check if the same trouble occurs again. OK: No DTCs are output. dtcs.description: USB Communication Malfunction This DTC is stored when a malfunction occurs in communication error with USB device or "iPod". HINT After the inspection is completed, clear the DTCs. PROCEDURE

steps.instruction: REPLACE "iPod" OR USB DEVICE (a) Disconnect the USB device or "iPod" from the No. 1 stereo jack adapter assembly. (b) Turn the engine switch off. HINT When this DTC has been stored, it is necessary to turn off the engine switch to make it possible for the vehicle to recognize a new device when it is connected. (c) Turn the engine switch on (ACC). (d) Connect a known good USB device or "iPod" to the No. 1 stereo jack adapter assembly. HINT * If the malfunction occurred when a USB device was in use, use another USB device for the inspection. If the malfunction occurred when an "iPod" was in use, use another "iPod" for the inspection. * Refer to System Description for compatible formats and versions    See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Radio, Stereo, and Compact Disc/Description and Operation/Audio and Visual System (w/ Multi-Display)/System Description. NEXT -- Continue to next step.

CHECK FOR DTC (a) Clear the DTCs    See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Radio, Stereo, and Compact Disc/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes/Audio and Visual System (w/ Multi-Display). (b) Check for DTCs. HINT If DTCs are stored frequently, replace the multi-media interface ECU. OK: No DTC is output. NG --  REPLACE MULTI-MEDIA INTERFACE ECU ASSEMBLY  See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Central Control Module/Service and Repair/Removal OK --  USB DEVICE OR "iPod" WAS DEFECTIVE Electro Multi Vision System

dtcs.description: Media Malfunction This DTC is output when signals from the disc in the display and navigation module display cannot be read. PROCEDURE

steps.instruction: CHECK DISC (a) Check that the disc is not deformed or cracked. OK: No deformation or cracks on the disc NG --  CHANGE DISC OK -- Continue to next step.

DISC CLEANING (a) Disc cleaning (1) If dirt is on the disc surface, wipe it clean with a soft cloth from the inside to the outside in a radial direction. NOTICE: Do not use a conventional record cleaner or antistatic preservative. NEXT -- Continue to next step.

REPLACE DISC WITH ANOTHER AND RECHECK (a) Replace the disc with another and recheck. (b) Clear the DTC    See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Radio, Stereo, and Compact Disc/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes/Audio and Visual System (w/ Multi-Display). dtcs.description: Harddisk Reading/Writing Malfunction(Low Temperature)

Harddisk Reading/Writing Malfunction(High Temperature) These DTCs are stored when the temperature is higher or lower than the operation range of the hard disc built into the display and navigation module display, and hard disc read/write errors occur more than a specified number of times. HINT The hard disc may not operate normally when the temperature is -20°C (-4°F) or lower, or 65°C (149°F) or higher. PROCEDURE

steps.instruction: CHECK CABIN Move the vehicle to an area that is at room temperature and allow the cabin temperature to adjust to between -20°C (-4°F) and 65°C (149°F). HINT * The hard disc may not operate normally at very high or low temperatures. * Perform the DTC check at room temperature. NEXT -- Continue to next step.

CHECK FOR DTC (a) Clear the DTC    See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Navigation System/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes/Navigation System (for HDD). (b) Check for DTCs and check if the same DTC is output again. OK: No DTCs are output. NG --  REPLACE HARD DISC  See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Navigation System/Navigation Display/Service and Repair/Removal and Replacement/Hard Disk Drive/Removal OK --  END

dtcs.description: A5-B19A3/A5: Short in Front Active Headrest Squib RH Circuit
A5 - Open in Front Active Headrest Squib RH Circuit
A5 - Short to GRD in Front Active Headrest Squib RH Circuit

A5 - Short to B+ in Front Active Headrest Squib RH Circuit The front active headrest squib RH circuit consists of the center airbag sensor and the active headrest inflator RH. This circuit instructs the SRS to deploy when deployment conditions are met. These DTCs are recorded when a malfunction is detected in the front active headrest squib RH circuit. HINT For vehicles not equipped with active headrest inflators: Vehicles that are not equipped with active headrest inflators still contain active headrest inflator circuits and use substitution parts in replace of the active headrest inflators. If a malfunction occurs in this active headrest inflator circuit, DTC B19A0, B19A1, B19A2 or

steps.instruction: CHECK ACTIVE HEADREST INFLATOR RH (FRONT ACTIVE HEADREST SQUIB RH) (a) Turn the engine switch off. (b) Disconnect the negative (-) terminal cable from the battery, and wait for at least 90 seconds. (c) Disconnect the connector from the active headrest inflator RH. (d) Connect the red wire side of SST (resistance: 2.1 Ohms) to connector E.

CHECK FRONT ACTIVE HEADREST SQUIB RH CIRCUIT (a) Turn the engine switch off. (b) Disconnect the negative (-) terminal cable from the battery, and wait for at least 90 seconds. (c) Disconnect SST from connector E. (d) Disconnect the connectors from the center airbag sensor. (e) Connect the negative (-) terminal cable to the battery, and wait for at least 2 seconds. (f) Turn the engine switch on (IG). (g) Measure the voltage according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard voltage: (h) Turn the engine switch off. (i) Disconnect the negative (-) terminal cable from the battery, and wait for at least 90 seconds. (j) Measure the resistance according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard resistance: (k) Release the activation prevention mechanism built into connector B    See: Restraints and Safety Systems/Air Bag Systems/Testing and Inspection/Initial Inspection and Diagnostic Overview/Diagnosis System. (l) Measure the resistance according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard resistance: Result A --  CHECK CENTER AIRBAG SENSOR B -- Continue to next step.

CHECK FRONT SEAT WIRE RH (a) Disconnect the floor wire connector from the rear seat wire. (b) Connect the negative (-) terminal cable to the battery, and wait for at least 2 seconds. (c) Turn the engine switch on (IG). (d) Measure the voltage according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard voltage: (e) Turn the engine switch off. (f) Disconnect the negative (-) terminal cable from the battery, and wait for at least 90 seconds. (g) Measure the resistance according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard resistance: (h) Release the activation prevention mechanism built into connector D    See: Restraints and Safety Systems/Air Bag Systems/Testing and Inspection/Initial Inspection and Diagnostic Overview/Diagnosis System. (i) Measure the resistance according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard resistance: NG --  REPLACE FRONT SEAT WIRE RH OK --  REPLACE FLOOR WIRE

CHECK CENTER AIRBAG SENSOR (a) Connect the connectors to the active headrest inflator RH and the center airbag sensor. (b) Connect the negative (-) terminal cable to the battery, and wait for at least 2 seconds. (c) Turn the engine switch on (IG), and wait for at least 60 seconds. (d) Clear the DTCs    See: Restraints and Safety Systems/Air Bag Systems/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes. (e) Turn the engine switch off. (f) Turn the engine switch on (IG), and wait for at least 60 seconds. (g) Check for DTCs    See: Restraints and Safety Systems/Air Bag Systems/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes. OK: DTC B19A0, B19A1, B19A2, B19A3 or A5 is not output. HINT Codes other than DTC B19A0, B19A1, B19A2, B19A3 and A5 may be output at this time, but they are not related to this check. NG --  REPLACE CENTER AIRBAG SENSOR ASSEMBLY  See: Relays and Modules/Relays and Modules - Restraints and Safety Systems/Air Bag Control Module/Service and Repair/Removal OK --  USE SIMULATION METHOD TO CHECK  See: Restraints and Safety Systems/Air Bag Systems/Testing and Inspection/Initial Inspection and Diagnostic Overview/Diagnosis System

dtcs.description: Multi-media Interface ECU Disconnected

No Response from Multi-media Interface ECU The display and navigation module display and multi-media interface ECU are connected by the AVC-LAN communication line. HINT * *: Even if no fault is present, this DTC may be stored depending on the battery condition or engine start voltage. * The display and navigation module display is the master unit. PROCEDURE

dtcs.description: LIN Communication Bus Malfunction The main body ECU (driver side junction block) monitors the LIN communication bus between the components related to the door and sliding roof intermittently. DTC B2325 is output when a malfunction in the LIN communication bus between the components related to the door and sliding roof is detected continuously 3 times. HINT *: w/ Sliding Roof System NOTICE: When using the Techstream with the engine switch off to troubleshoot: Connect the Techstream to the vehicle, and turn a courtesy switch on and off at 1.5 second intervals until communication between the Techstream and vehicle begins. HINT When DTC B2325 and a LIN communication stop DTC are output simultaneously, first perform the troubleshooting for the LIN communication stop DTC. Then perform the troubleshooting for DTC B2325. PROCEDURE

dtcs.description: Wireless Door Lock Tuner Circuit Malfunction The electrical key antenna is used to receive electrical waves relating to the entry functions of the smart access system with push-button start. The certification ECU decodes the requested smart access system with push-button start operation by identifying a key code based on electric waves received via the electrical key antenna and the door control receiver. The door control receiver receives a signal from the door control transmitter and sends signals to the main body ECU through the certification ECU. The certification ECU then sends a command, according to the requested operation, to each ECU (ex. if door lock operation is requested, the ECU sends a door lock command to the main body ECU). This DTC is output when one of the following occurs: 1) short in RDA or RSSI ground between the certification ECU and the door control receiver, or

steps.instruction: CHECK HARNESS AND CONNECTOR (CERTIFICATION ECU - DOOR CONTROL RECEIVER) (a) Disconnect the L15 ECU connector. (b) Disconnect the R37 receiver connector. (c) Measure the resistance according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard resistance: NG --  REPAIR OR REPLACE HARNESS OR CONNECTOR OK -- Continue to next step.

short in RDA or RSSI ground when RC0 (5 V) output from the ECU is off. NOTICE: When replacing or inspecting the door control receiver and wire harness, do not change the position or length of the wire harness. If the wire harness is too close to the door control receiver, entry and wireless function performance may be affected. PROCEDURE

dtcs.description: 11 - Room Temperature Sensor Circuit The room temperature sensor for the front seat is installed in the instrument panel to detect the room temperature and control the heater and air conditioner "AUTO" mode. The resistance of the room temperature sensor changes in accordance with the room temperature. As the temperature decreases, the resistance increases. As the temperature increases, the resistance decreases. The air conditioning amplifier applies voltage (5 V) to the room temperature sensor and reads voltage changes as the resistance of the room temperature sensor changes. This sensor also sends the appropriate signals to the air conditioning amplifier. PROCEDURE

dtcs.description: 12 - Ambient Temperature Sensor Circuit The ambient temperature sensor is installed in the front part of the condenser to detect the ambient temperature and control the air conditioner "AUTO" mode. The sensor connected to the front light ECU (front controller) detects fluctuations in the ambient temperature. This data is used for controlling the room temperature. The sensor sends a signal to the air conditioning amplifier via the front light ECU (front controller). The resistance of the ambient temperature sensor changes in accordance with the ambient temperature. As the temperature decreases, the resistance increases. As the temperature increases, the resistance decreases. The front light ECU (front controller) applies voltage (5 V) to the ambient temperature sensor and reads voltage changes as the resistance of the ambient temperature sensor changes. The front light ECU (front controller) sends the read signal to the air conditioning amplifier via the CAN communication line. HINT This circuit uses the CAN communication line. Before performing the inspection, check that the CAN communication system is functioning normally. PROCEDURE

dtcs.description: 13 - Evaporator Temperature Sensor Circuit The No. 1 cooler thermistor (evaporator temperature sensor) is installed on the evaporator in the air conditioner unit to detect the cooled air temperature that has passed through the evaporator and control the air conditioning. It sends appropriate signals to the air conditioning amplifier. The resistance of the No. 1 cooler thermistor (evaporator temperature sensor) changes in accordance with the cooled air temperature that has passed through the evaporator. As the temperature decreases, the resistance increases. As the temperature increases, the resistance decreases. The air conditioning amplifier applies voltage (5 V) to the No. 1 cooler thermistor (evaporator temperature sensor) and reads voltage changes as the resistance of the No. 1 cooler thermistor (evaporator temperature sensor) changes. This sensor is used for frost prevention. PROCEDURE

dtcs.description: 18 - Exhaust Gas Sensor Circuit The exhaust gas sensor, which is installed on the front of the condenser, automatically changes to FRESH, FRESH / RECIRCULATION, or RECIRCULATION. The exhaust gas sensor detects CO and HC in the emission gas and transmits signals to the front light ECU (front controller). The front light ECU (front controller) sends the read signal to the air conditioning amplifier via CAN communication line. HINT This circuit uses the CAN communication line. Before performing the inspection, check that the CAN communication system is functioning normally. PROCEDURE

dtcs.description: 21 - Solar Sensor Circuit (Passenger Side) The solar sensor, which is installed on the upper side of the instrument panel, detects sunlight and controls the air conditioning in AUTO mode. The output current from the solar sensor varies according to the amount of sunlight. When the sunlight increases, the output current increases. As the sunlight decreases, the output current decreases. The air conditioning amplifier detects output current from the solar sensor. dtcs.description: 23 - Pressure Sensor Circuit This DTC is output when refrigerant pressure is extremely low (0.19 MPa (2.0 kgf/cm2, 28 psi) or less) or extremely high (3.14 MPa (32.0 kgf/cm2, 455 psi) or more). The pressure sensor, which is installed on the pipe of the high pressure side to detect refrigerant pressure, outputs the refrigerant pressure signal to the front light ECU (front controller). The front light ECU (front controller) sends the read signal to the air conditioning amplifier via the CAN communication line. The air conditioning amplifier converts the signals to pressure according to the sensor characteristics to control the compressor. HINT * Be sure to check the refrigerant volume first when this DTC is output because this DTC can also be output if there is no refrigerant in the cycle. * This circuit uses the CAN communication line. Before performing the inspection, check that the CAN communication system is functioning normally. PROCEDURE

dtcs.description: 41 - Air Mix Damper Control Servo Motor Circuit (Passenger Side) The air mix damper servo (for passenger side HOT) sends pulse signals to inform the air conditioning amplifier of the damper position. dtcs.description: 49 - Rear Air Outlet Damper Control Servo Motor Circuit The rear outlet damper servo (for LH side) sends pulse signals to inform the air conditioning amplifier of the damper position. The air conditioning amplifier activates the motor (normal or reverse) based on these signals to move the rear outlet damper (for LH side) to the appropriate position, which controls the air outlet modes. HINT Confirm that no mechanical problem is present because this diagnostic code can be output when either a damper link or damper is mechanically locked. NOTICE: * If this DTC is output, perform the following procedures before beginning the INSPECTION PROCEDURE. (1) Change the air outlet mode position for the rear LH and RH seats to "FOOT". (2) Turn the engine switch off, and wait approximately 90 seconds. (3) Turn the engine switch on (IG). (4) Clear DTCs See: Heating and Air Conditioning/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes/DTC Check / Clear. (5) With the air outlet mode position for the rear LH and RH seats on "FOOT", wait approximately 45 seconds. (6) Check for DTCs See: Heating and Air Conditioning/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes/DTC Check / Clear. If this DTC is not output, there is no malfunction. If this DTC is output, perform the INSPECTION PROCEDURE below. PROCEDURE

dtcs.description: 61 - Emission Gas NOx Sensor Circuit The exhaust gas sensor, which is installed on the front of the condenser, automatically changes to FRESH, FRESH / RECIRCULATION, or RECIRCULATION. The exhaust gas sensor detects NOx in the emission gas and transmits signals to the front light ECU (front controller). The front light ECU (front controller) sends the read signal to the air conditioning amplifier via CAN communication line. HINT This circuit uses the CAN communication line. Before performing the inspection, check that the CAN communication system is functioning normally. PROCEDURE

dtcs.description: 68 - Rear Passenger Side Room Temperature Sensor Circuit The rear room temperature sensor RH is installed in the rear door trim board RH to detect the rear room temperature and control the heater and air conditioner "AUTO" mode. The resistance of the rear room temperature sensor changes in accordance with the rear room temperature for RH seat. As the temperature decreases, the resistance increases. As the temperature increases, the resistance decreases. The rear door ECU RH sends the read signal to the air conditioning amplifier via the CAN communication line. The rear door ECU RH applies voltage (5 V) to the rear room temperature sensor and reads voltage changes as the resistance of the rear room temperature sensor changes. This sensor also sends the appropriate signals to the rear door ECU RH. HINT This circuit uses the CAN communication line. Before performing the inspection, check that the CAN communication system is functioning normally. PROCEDURE

steps.instruction: READ VALUE USING TECHSTREAM (REAR ROOM TEMPERATURE SENSOR (for RH)) (a) Use the Data List to check if the rear room temperature sensor (for RH seat) is functioning properly. (b) Measure the resistance according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard resistance: NOTICE: Even slightly touching the sensor may change the resistance value. Be sure to hold the connector of the sensor. HINT As the temperature increases, the resistance decreases (see the graph). NG --  REPLACE REAR ROOM TEMPERATURE SENSOR RH  See: Heating and Air Conditioning/Cabin Temperature Sensor / Switch/Service and Repair/Room Temperature Sensor (for Rear Side)/Removal OK -- Continue to next step.

(2) Turn the engine switch off, and wait approximately 90 seconds. (3) Turn the engine switch on (IG). (4) Clear DTCs See: Heating and Air Conditioning/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes/DTC Check / Clear. (5) With the air outlet mode position for the rear LH and RH seats on "FOOT", wait approximately 45 seconds. (6) Check for DTCs See: Heating and Air Conditioning/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes/DTC Check / Clear. If this DTC is not output, there is no malfunction. If this DTC is output, perform the INSPECTION PROCEDURE below. PROCEDURE

dtcs.description: 92 - Infrared Ray Sensor Communication Malfunction This circuit consists of the infrared ray sensor and the air conditioning amplifier. The infrared ray sensor, which is installed on the rear roof, detects human body temperature and controls the heater and air conditioner "AUTO" mode in 4 zone. Signals are transmitted to the air conditioning amplifier via LIN communication line. This DTC is output when LIN communication between the infrared ray sensor and air conditioning amplifier stop for 10 seconds or more. If the LIN communication system malfunctions, the air conditioning amplifier does not operate even if the infrared ray sensor is normal. HINT For vehicles with infrared ray sensor only. PROCEDURE

(b) Operate the infrared ray sensor to check that it functions normally. OK: Infrared ray sensor function operates normally. NG --  REPLACE AIR CONDITIONING AMPLIFIER ASSEMBLY  See: Heating and Air Conditioning/Control Module HVAC/Service and Repair/Removal OK --  END (INFRARED RAY SENSOR IS FAULTY) Air Conditioning System

As a result, when the air conditioner is operating, the temperature of the air blowing toward the driver's head area is controlled. dtcs.description: DVD-ROM Deck Disconnected The display and navigation module display and navigation module board are connected by the IEEE cable. NOTICE: Inspect the fuses for circuits related to this system before performing the following inspection procedure. PROCEDURE

dtcs.description: Manual (SOS) Switch Red Indicator Malfunction This DTC is stored when the DCM (telematics transceiver) detects an open or short in the manual (SOS) switch red indicator circuit of the manual (SOS) switch. The manual (SOS) switch red indicator illuminates for 2 seconds and goes off when the engine switch is turned on (IG). If a malfunction in the Safety Connect System is detected, the manual (SOS) switch red indicator will illuminate. However, the manual (SOS) switch red indicator may not illuminate when this DTC is stored. HINT The manual (SOS) switch red indicator will not illuminate to indicate that another DTC is stored. Therefore a voice guidance will indicate that other DTCs are stored. PROCEDURE

steps.instruction: CHECK FOR DTC (a) Turn the engine switch off. (b) Connect the Techstream to the DLC3. (c) Turn the engine switch on (IG) and wait for 10 seconds. (d) Perform "Health Check" and check for current DTCs    See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Emergency Contact Module/Testing and Inspection/Initial Inspection and Diagnostic Overview/Safety Connect System/Health Check. INSPECT MAP LIGHT ASSEMBLY (RED INDICATOR) (a) Disconnect the X4 and X6 map light assembly connectors. (b) Connect 2 dry-cell batteries (1.5 V each) in series. (c) Connect the positive (+) lead of the battery to terminal X6-4 (IND1) and the negative (-) lead to terminal X4-16 (GND9) of the map light assembly connectors. (d) Check if the manual (SOS) switch red indicator comes on. OK: Red indicator comes on. (c) Measure the resistance according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard Resistance: Text in Illustration NG --  REPAIR OR REPLACE HARNESS OR CONNECTOR OK -- Continue to next step.

REPLACE DCM (TELEMATICS TRANSCEIVER) (a) Replace the DCM (telematics transceiver)    See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Emergency Contact Module/Service and Repair/Removal. Result B --  REPLACE DCM (TELEMATICS TRANSCEIVER) A -- Continue to next step.

CHECK MAP LIGHT ASSEMBLY (RED INDICATOR INPUT VOLTAGE) (a) Remove the map light assembly with the connector connected    See: Lighting and Horns/Map Light/Service and Repair/Removal and Replacement/Removal. (b) Connect the positive lead of a voltmeter to terminal X6-4 (IND1) and the negative lead to terminal X4-16 (GND9). (c) Measure the voltage. OK: 1.0 to 8.5 V in 2 seconds after the engine switch is turned on (IG). 0 V when the engine switch is turned off. Text in Illustration NG --  CHECK DCM (TELEMATICS TRANSCEIVER) (RED INDICATOR OUTPUT VOLTAGE) OK --  REPLACE MAP LIGHT ASSEMBLY  See: Lighting and Horns/Map Light/Service and Repair/Removal and Replacement/Removal

CHECK DCM (TELEMATICS TRANSCEIVER) (RED INDICATOR OUTPUT VOLTAGE) (a) Remove the DCM (telematics transceiver) with the connector connected    See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Emergency Contact Module/Service and Repair/Removal. dtcs.description: Manual (SOS) Switch Green Indicator Malfunction This DTC is stored when the DCM (telematics transceiver) detects an open or short in the manual (SOS) switch green indicator circuit of the manual (SOS) switch. The manual (SOS) switch green indicator illuminates after the engine switch is turned on (IG). If the Safety Connect System is not active, the manual (SOS) switch green indicator will turn off. If the Safety Connect System is active, the manual (SOS) switch green indicator will blink while the system is in communicating with the call center. PROCEDURE

steps.instruction: CHECK FOR DTC (a) Turn the engine switch off. (b) Connect the Techstream to the DLC3. (c) Turn the engine switch on (IG) and wait for 10 seconds. (d) Perform "Health Check" and check for current DTCs    See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Emergency Contact Module/Testing and Inspection/Initial Inspection and Diagnostic Overview/Safety Connect System/Health Check. Result B --  CHECK MAP LIGHT ASSEMBLY (MANUAL [SOS] SWITCH GREEN INDICATOR CONDITION) A -- Continue to next step.

(b) Measure the resistance according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard Resistance: Text in Illustration NG --  REPAIR OR REPLACE HARNESS OR CONNECTOR OK -- Continue to next step.

INSPECT MAP LIGHT ASSEMBLY (GREEN INDICATOR) (a) Disconnect the X4 and X6 map light assembly connectors. (b) Connect 2 dry-cell batteries (1.5 V each) in series. (c) Connect the positive (+) lead of the battery to terminal X6-5 (IND2) and the negative (-) lead to terminal X4-16 (GND9) of the map light assembly connectors. (d) Check if the manual (SOS) switch green indicator comes on. OK: Green indicator comes on. dtcs.description: Telephone Microphone Error This DTC is stored when the DCM (telematics transceiver) detects a malfunction in the telephone microphone assembly circuit. PROCEDURE

steps.instruction: CHECK FOR DTC (a) Turn the engine switch off. (b) Connect the Techstream to the DLC3. (c) Turn the engine switch on (IG) and wait for 10 seconds. (d) Perform "Health Check" and check for current DTCs    See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Emergency Contact Module/Testing and Inspection/Initial Inspection and Diagnostic Overview/Safety Connect System/Health Check. Result B --  CHECK FOR INTERMITTENT PROBLEMS  See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Antitheft and Alarm Systems/Diagrams/Diagnostic Aids/How To Troubleshoot ECU Controlled Systems/How To Proceed With Troubleshooting A -- Continue to next step.

CHECK MAP LIGHT ASSEMBLY (TELEPHONE MICROPHONE POWER SOURCE) (a) Remove the map light assembly with the connector connected    See: Lighting and Horns/Map Light/Service and Repair/Removal and Replacement/Removal. (b) Measure the voltage according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard Voltage: Text in Illustration NG --  CHECK DCM (TELEMATICS TRANSCEIVER) (TELEPHONE MICROPHONE POWER SOURCE) OK -- Continue to next step.

CHECK HARNESS AND CONNECTOR (DCM - MAP LIGHT ASSEMBLY) (a) Disconnect the L111 DCM (telematics transceiver) connector. (b) Disconnect the X4 map light assembly connector. (c) Measure the resistance according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard Resistance: Text in Illustration NG --  REPAIR OR REPLACE HARNESS OR CONNECTOR OK -- Continue to next step.

REPLACE TELEPHONE MICROPHONE ASSEMBLY (a) Replace the telephone microphone assembly with a normally functioning one and check that the same problem does not occur again    See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Cellular Phone/Cellular Phone Microphone/Service and Repair/Telephone Microphone/Removal. OK: The system returns to normal. NG --  REPLACE MAP LIGHT ASSEMBLY  See: Lighting and Horns/Map Light/Service and Repair/Removal and Replacement/Removal OK --  END

CHECK DCM (TELEMATICS TRANSCEIVER) (TELEPHONE MICROPHONE POWER SOURCE) (a) Remove the DCM (telematics transceiver) with the connector connected    See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Emergency Contact Module/Service and Repair/Removal. (b) Measure the voltage according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard Voltage: Text in Illustration NG --  REPLACE DCM (TELEMATICS TRANSCEIVER) OK --  REPAIR OR REPLACE HARNESS OR CONNECTOR

REPLACE DCM (TELEMATICS TRANSCEIVER) (a) Replace the DCM (telematics transceiver)    See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Emergency Contact Module/Service and Repair/Removal. NOTICE: * The engine switch must be off. * Do not replace the DCM (telematics transceiver) with one from another vehicle. NEXT --  PERFORM DCM ACTIVATION  See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Emergency Contact Module/Testing and Inspection/Scan Tool Testing and Procedures/DCM Activation

dtcs.description: Map Disk Reading Malfunction This DTC is stored when the display and navigation module display cannot read the map disc. PROCEDURE

steps.instruction: CHECK MAP DISC (a) Check that the map disc is not deformed or cracked. OK: No deformations or cracks in the map disc. NG --  REPLACE MAP DISC OK -- Continue to next step.

DISC CLEANING (a) If dirt is on the disc surface, wipe it clean with a soft cloth from the inside to the outside in a radial direction. NOTICE: Do not use a conventional record cleaner or antistatic preservative. NEXT -- Continue to next step.

CHECK FOR DTC (a) Clear the DTCs    See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Navigation System/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes/Navigation System (for DVD). (b) Check for DTCs and check if the same trouble occurs again. OK: No DTC is output. NG --  REPLACE MAP DISC OK --  END

REPLACE MAP DISC (a) Temporarily replace the map disc with a new or normally functioning one. (b) Clear the DTCs    See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Navigation System/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes/Navigation System (for DVD). (c) Recheck for DTCs and check if the same trouble occurs again. OK: No DTC is output. NG --  REPLACE DISPLAY AND NAVIGATION MODULE DISPLAY  See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Navigation System/Navigation Display/Service and Repair/Removal and Replacement/Multi-Display (w/ Navigation System)/Removal OK --  END

dtcs.description: GPS Signal Unreceived If GPS satellite signals cannot be acquired for 16 consecutive kilometers (10 consecutive miles), this DTC is stored. HINT If DTC B15C0 or B15C1 is output at the same time as DTC B1583, perform troubleshooting for DTC B15C0 or B15C1 first. PROCEDURE

steps.instruction: CHECK TELEPHONE AND GPS ANTENNA ASSEMBLY (a) Check that there are no problems with the telephone and GPS antenna. OK: The antenna is not excessively dirty and there are no objects blocking signal reception. NG --  REMOVE FOREIGN OBJECT OK -- Continue to next step.

REPLACE TELEPHONE AND GPS ANTENNA (a) Replace the telephone and GPS antenna    See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Antenna/Antenna, Phone/Service and Repair/Telephone and GPS Antenna/Removal. NEXT -- Continue to next step.

CLEAR DTC (a) Clear the current and past DTCs    See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Emergency Contact Module/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes. NEXT -- Continue to next step.

CHECK FOR DTC (a) Drive the vehicle for at least 24 km (15 miles). HINT The manual (SOS) switch red indicator will illuminate at approximately 16 km (10 miles) if the problem still exists. (b) Turn the engine switch off. (c) Connect the Techstream to the DLC3. (d) Turn the engine switch on (IG) and wait for 10 seconds. (e) Perform "Health Check" and check for current and past DTCs    See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Emergency Contact Module/Testing and Inspection/Initial Inspection and Diagnostic Overview/Safety Connect System/Health Check. HINT Turning the engine switch off will clear the current DTC. If the vehicle is driven approximately 16 km (10 miles) and the problem is still present, the DTC will be stored again. Result B --  END A -- Continue to next step.

REPLACE DCM (TELEMATICS TRANSCEIVER) (a) Replace the DCM (telematics transceiver)    See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Emergency Contact Module/Service and Repair/Removal. NOTICE: * The engine switch must be off. * Do not replace the DCM (telematics transceiver) with one from another vehicle. NEXT --  PERFORM DCM ACTIVATION  See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Emergency Contact Module/Testing and Inspection/Scan Tool Testing and Procedures/DCM Activation Audio and Visual System (w/ Multi-Display)

dtcs.description: USB Media Malfunction This DTC is stored when a malfunction occurs in a connected device. HINT After the inspection is completed, clear the DTCs. PROCEDURE

steps.instruction: REPLACE USB DEVICE OR "iPod" (a) Disconnect the USB device or "iPod" from the No. 1 stereo jack adapter assembly. (b) Turn the engine switch off. HINT When this DTC has been stored, it is necessary to turn off the engine switch to make it possible for the vehicle to recognize a new device when it is connected. (c) Turn the engine switch on (ACC). (d) Connect a known good USB device or "iPod" to the No. 1 stereo jack adapter assembly. HINT * If the malfunction occurred when a USB device was in use, use another USB device for the inspection. If the malfunction occurred when an "iPod" was in use, use another "iPod" for the inspection. * Refer to System Description for compatible formats and versions    See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Radio, Stereo, and Compact Disc/Description and Operation/Audio and Visual System (w/ Multi-Display)/System Description. NEXT -- Continue to next step.

CHECK FOR DTC (a) Clear the DTCs    See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Radio, Stereo, and Compact Disc/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes/Audio and Visual System (w/ Multi-Display). (b) Check for DTCs. HINT If DTCs are stored frequently, replace the multi-media interface ECU. OK: No DTC is output. NG -- REPLACE MULTI-MEDIA INTERFACE ECU  See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Central Control Module/Service and Repair/Removal OK -- USB DEVICE FORMAT WAS INCOMPATIBLE, "iPod" VERSION WAS INCOMPATIBLE, OR USB DEVICE OR "iPod" WAS DEFECTIVE Electro Multi Vision System

dtcs.description: 83, B1612/83 or B1613/83 is recorded when a malfunction is detected in the front airbag sensor RH circuit. NOTICE: After the engine switch is turned off, the HDD navigation system requires approximately 6 minutes to record various types of memory and settings. As a result, after turning the engine switch off, wait 6 minutes or more before disconnecting the cable from the negative (-) battery terminal. PROCEDURE

steps.instruction: CHECK CONNECTION OF CONNECTORS (a) Turn the engine switch off. (b) Disconnect the negative (-) terminal cable from the battery, and wait for at least 90 seconds. (c) Check that the connectors are properly connected to the center airbag sensor and the front airbag sensor RH. OK: The connectors are properly connected. NG --  CONNECT CONNECTORS PROPERLY OK -- Continue to next step.

CHECK CONNECTORS (a) Disconnect the connectors from the center airbag sensor and front airbag sensor RH. (b) Check that the connectors (on the center airbag sensor side and front airbag sensor RH side) are not damaged. OK: The connectors are not deformed or damaged. NG --  REPLACE HARNESS AND CONNECTOR OK -- Continue to next step.

CHECK FRONT AIRBAG SENSOR RH CIRCUIT (a) Connect the negative (-) terminal cable to the battery, and wait for at least 2 seconds. (b) Turn the engine switch on (IG). (c) Measure the voltage according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard voltage: (d) Turn the engine switch off. (e) Disconnect the negative (-) terminal cable from the battery, and wait for at least 90 seconds. (f) Using a service wire, connect terminals 29 (+SR) and 27 (-SR) of connector B. NOTICE: Do not forcibly insert a service wire into the terminals of the connector when connecting the wire. (g) Measure the resistance according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard resistance: (h) Disconnect the service wire from connector B. (i) Measure the resistance according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard resistance: NG --  CHECK INSTRUMENT PANEL WIRE (CENTER AIRBAG SENSOR - ENGINE ROOM MAIN WIRE) OK -- Continue to next step.

CHECK FRONT AIRBAG SENSOR RH (a) Connect the connectors to the center airbag sensor. (b) Interchange the front airbag sensor LH with RH and connect the connectors to them. (c) Connect the negative (-) terminal cable to the battery, and wait for at least 2 seconds. (d) Turn the engine switch on (IG), and wait for at least 60 seconds. (e) Clear the DTCs stored in the memory    See: Restraints and Safety Systems/Air Bag Systems/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes. (f) Turn the engine switch off. (g) Turn the engine switch on (IG), and wait for at least 60 seconds. (h) Check for DTCs    See: Restraints and Safety Systems/Air Bag Systems/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes. Result HINT Codes other than DTC B1603, B1608, B1612, B1613, B1617 and B1618 or 84 may be output at this time, but they are not related to this check. A --  USE SIMULATION METHOD TO CHECK  See: Restraints and Safety Systems/Air Bag Systems/Testing and Inspection/Initial Inspection and Diagnostic Overview/Diagnosis System B --  REPLACE FRONT AIRBAG SENSOR RH  See: Restraints and Safety Systems/Air Bag Systems/Impact Sensor/Service and Repair/Front Airbag Sensor/Components C --  REPLACE CENTER AIRBAG SENSOR ASSEMBLY  See: Relays and Modules/Relays and Modules - Restraints and Safety Systems/Air Bag Control Module/Service and Repair/Components

CHECK INSTRUMENT PANEL WIRE (CENTER AIRBAG SENSOR - ENGINE ROOM MAIN WIRE) (a) Disconnect the instrument panel wire connector from the engine room main wire. (b) Connect the negative (-) terminal cable to the battery, and wait for at least 2 seconds. (c) Turn the engine switch on (IG). (d) Measure the voltage according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard voltage: (e) Turn the engine switch off. (f) Disconnect the negative (-) terminal cable from the battery, and wait for at least 90 seconds. (g) Using a service wire, connect terminal 27 (-SR) and 29 (+SR) of connector B. NOTICE: Do not forcibly insert a service wire into the terminals of the connector when connecting the wire. (h) Measure the resistance according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard resistance: (i) Disconnect the service wire from connector B. (j) Measure the resistance according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard resistance: NG --  REPLACE INSTRUMENT PANEL WIRE OK --  REPLACE ENGINE ROOM MAIN WIRE

dtcs.description: 83 - Lost Communication with Front Airbag Sensor RH

83 - Front Airbag Sensor RH Initialization Incomplete The front airbag sensor RH circuit consists of the center airbag sensor and front airbag sensor RH. The front airbag sensor RH detects impacts to the vehicle and sends signals to the center airbag sensor to determine if the airbag should be deployed.

dtcs.description: 84 - Lost Communication with Front Airbag Sensor LH

84 - Front Airbag Sensor LH Initialization Incomplete The front airbag sensor LH circuit consists of the center airbag sensor and front airbag sensor LH. The front airbag sensor LH detects impacts to the vehicle and sends signals to the center airbag sensor to determine if the airbag should be deployed.

13 is recorded when a malfunction is detected in the front airbag sensor RH circuit. NOTICE: After the engine switch is turned off, the HDD navigation system requires approximately 6 minutes to record various types of memory and settings. As a result, after turning the engine switch off, wait 6 minutes or more before disconnecting the cable from the negative (-) battery terminal. PROCEDURE

steps.instruction: CHECK FRONT AIRBAG SENSOR RH (a) Turn the engine switch off. (b) Disconnect the negative (-) terminal cable from the battery, and wait for at least 90 seconds. (c) Interchange the front airbag sensor LH with RH and connect the connectors to them. (d) Connect the negative (-) terminal cable to the battery, and wait for at least 2 seconds. (e) Turn the engine switch on (IG), and wait for at least 60 seconds. (f) Clear the DTCs stored in the memory    See: Restraints and Safety Systems/Air Bag Systems/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes. (g) Turn the engine switch off. (h) Turn the engine switch on (IG), and wait for at least 60 seconds. steps.instruction: CHECK FRONT AIRBAG SENSOR LH (a) Turn the engine switch off. (b) Disconnect the negative (-) terminal cable from the battery, and wait for at least 90 seconds. (c) Interchange the front airbag sensor RH with LH and connect the connectors to them. (d) Connect the negative (-) terminal cable to the battery, and wait for at least 2 seconds. (e) Turn the engine switch on (IG), and wait for at least 60 seconds. (f) Clear the DTCs stored in the memory    See: Restraints and Safety Systems/Air Bag Systems/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes. (g) Turn the engine switch off. dtcs.description: 21 or B1625/22 is recorded when a malfunction is detected in the side airbag sensor LH or RH circuit. for DTC B1622 and B1627: The circuit for the side collision sensor RH or LH (to determine deployment of the front seat side airbag RH or LH, rear seat side airbag RH or LH, and curtain shield airbag RH or LH) is composed of the center airbag sensor, side airbag sensor RH or LH, and rear airbag sensor RH or LH. The side airbag sensor RH or LH, and rear airbag sensor RH or LH detect impacts to the vehicle and send signals to the center airbag sensor to determine if the airbag should be deployed.

82 - Lost communication with Side Airbag Sensor LH for DTC B1620 and B1625: The side airbag sensor LH and RH consists of the safing sensor, the diagnostic circuit, the lateral deceleration sensor, etc. If the center airbag sensor receives signals from the lateral deceleration sensor, it determines whether the SRS should be activated.

or B1627 is recorded when a malfunction is detected in the circuit for the side collision sensor RH or LH (to determine deployment of the front seat side airbag RH or LH, rear seat side airbag RH or LH, and curtain shield airbag RH or LH). NOTICE: After the engine switch is turned off, the HDD navigation system requires approximately 6 minutes to record various types of memory and settings. As a result, after turning the engine switch off, wait 6 minutes or more before disconnecting the cable from the negative (-) battery terminal. PROCEDURE

82 - Side Satellite Sensor Bus LH Initialization Incomplete The circuit for the side collision sensor LH or RH (to determine deployment of the front seat side airbag LH or RH and curtain shield airbag LH or RH) is composed of the center airbag sensor, side airbag sensor LH or RH, and rear airbag sensor LH or RH. The side airbag sensor LH or RH and rear airbag sensor LH or RH detect impacts to the vehicle and send signals to the center airbag sensor to determine if the airbag should be deployed.

81 or B1638/82 is recorded when a malfunction is detected in the circuit for the rear collision sensor LH or RH (to determine deployment of the rear seat side airbag LH or RH and curtain shield airbag LH or RH).

81 or B1648/82 is recorded when a malfunction is detected in the circuit for the rear collision sensor LH or RH (to determine deployment of the front and rear seat side airbag LH or RH and curtain shield airbag LH or RH). NOTICE: After the engine switch is turned off, the HDD navigation system requires approximately 6 minutes to record various types of memory and settings. As a result, after turning the engine switch off, wait 6 minutes or more before disconnecting the cable from the negative (-) battery terminal. PROCEDURE

steps.instruction: CHECK PAST DTC (a) Turn the engine switch on (IG), and wait for at least 60 seconds. (b) Turn the engine switch off. (c) Turn the engine switch on (IG), and wait for at least 60 seconds. (d) Check the past DTCs    See: Restraints and Safety Systems/Air Bag Systems/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes. Result: A --  GO TO DTC B1622  See: Diagnostic Trouble Code Descriptions/Airbag System B --  GO TO DTC B1632  See: B1632 C -- Continue to next step.

CHECK FOR DTC (a) Turn the engine switch off. HINT If a communication error occurs, DTCs for both the LH and RH sides will be stored simultaneously. To identify the malfunctioning area, turn the engine switch off and then on (IG) again. (b) Turn the engine switch on (IG), and wait for at least 60 seconds. (c) Check for DTCs    See: Restraints and Safety Systems/Air Bag Systems/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes. Result: A --  USE SIMULATION METHOD TO CHECK  See: Restraints and Safety Systems/Air Bag Systems/Testing and Inspection/Initial Inspection and Diagnostic Overview/Diagnosis System B --  CHECK CONNECTION OF CONNECTORS C -- Continue to next step.

CHECK CONNECTION OF CONNECTORS (a) Turn the engine switch off. (b) Disconnect the negative (-) terminal cable from the battery, and wait for at least 90 seconds. (c) Check that the connectors are properly connected to the center airbag sensor and side airbag sensor RH. OK: The connectors are properly connected. NG --  CONNECT CONNECTORS PROPERLY OK -- Continue to next step.

CHECK CONNECTORS (a) Disconnect the connectors from the center airbag sensor and side airbag sensor RH. (b) Check that the connectors (on the center airbag sensor side and side airbag sensor RH side) are not damaged. OK: The connectors are not deformed or damaged. NG --  REPLACE FLOOR WIRE OK -- Continue to next step.

CHECK FLOOR WIRE (CENTER AIRBAG SENSOR - SIDE AIRBAG SENSOR RH) (a) Connect the negative (-) terminal cable to the battery, and wait for at least 2 seconds. (b) Turn the engine switch on (IG). (c) Measure the voltage according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard voltage: (d) Turn the engine switch off. (e) Disconnect the negative (-) terminal cable from the battery, and wait for at least 90 seconds. (f) Using a service wire, connect terminals 17 (BBR+) and 18 (BBR-) of connector B. NOTICE: Do not forcibly insert a service wire into the terminals of the connector when connecting the wire. (g) Measure the resistance according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard resistance: (h) Disconnect the service wire from connector B. (i) Measure the resistance according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard resistance: NG --  REPLACE FLOOR WIRE OK -- Continue to next step.

CHECK CONNECTION OF CONNECTORS (a) Check that the connector is properly connected to the rear airbag sensor RH. OK: The connector is properly connected. NG --  CONNECT CONNECTOR PROPERLY OK -- Continue to next step.

CHECK CONNECTORS (a) Disconnect the connectors from the side airbag sensor RH and rear airbag sensor RH. (b) Check that the connector (on the rear airbag sensor RH side) is not damaged. OK: The connector is not deformed or damaged. NG --  REPLACE FLOOR WIRE OK -- Continue to next step.

CHECK FLOOR WIRE (SIDE AIRBAG SENSOR RH - REAR AIRBAG SENSOR RH) (a) Connect the negative (-) terminal cable to the battery, and wait for at least 2 seconds. (b) Turn the engine switch on (IG). (c) Measure the voltage according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard voltage: (d) Turn the engine switch off. (e) Disconnect the negative (-) terminal cable from the battery, and wait for at least 90 seconds. (f) Using a service wire, connect terminals 2 (BCR+) and 1 (BCR-) of connector C. NOTICE: Do not forcibly insert a service wire into the terminals of the connector when connecting the wire. (g) Measure the resistance according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard resistance: (h) Disconnect the service wire from connector C. (i) Measure the resistance according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard resistance: NG --  REPLACE FLOOR WIRE OK -- Continue to next step.

CHECK SIDE AIRBAG SENSOR RH (a) Interchange the side airbag sensor LH with RH and connect the connectors to them. (b) Connect the negative (-) terminal cable to the battery, and wait for at least 2 seconds. (c) Turn the engine switch on (IG), and wait for at least 60 seconds.
