dtcs.description: 32 is recorded when a malfunction is detected in the occupant classification system circuit. NOTICE: After the engine switch is turned off, the HDD navigation system requires approximately 6 minutes to record various types of memory and settings. As a result, after turning the engine switch off, wait 6 minutes or more before disconnecting the cable from the negative (-) battery terminal. PROCEDURE

steps.instruction: CHECK FOR DTC (OCCUPANT CLASSIFICATION ECU) (a) Turn the engine switch on (IG), and wait for at least 10 seconds. (b) Check for the DTCs of the occupant classification ECU    See: Restraints and Safety Systems/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes. OK: DTC is not output. NG --  REPAIR CIRCUITS INDICATED BY OUTPUT DTCS  See: Diagnostic Trouble Code Descriptions/Occupant Classification System OK -- Continue to next step.

CHECK FOR DTC (CENTER AIRBAG SENSOR) (a) Turn the engine switch on (IG), and wait for at least 60 seconds. (b) Clear the DTCs    See: Restraints and Safety Systems/Air Bag Systems/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes. (c) Turn the engine switch off. (d) Turn the engine switch on (IG), and wait for at least 60 seconds. (e) Check for DTCs    See: Restraints and Safety Systems/Air Bag Systems/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes. OK:

CHECK CONNECTION OF CONNECTORS (a) Turn the engine switch off. (b) Disconnect the negative (-) terminal cable from the battery, and wait for at least 90 seconds. (c) Check that the connectors are properly connected to the center airbag sensor and the occupant classification ECU. OK: The connectors are properly connected. NG --  CONNECT CONNECTORS PROPERLY OK -- Continue to next step.

CHECK CONNECTORS (a) Disconnect the connectors from the center airbag sensor and the occupant classification ECU. (b) Check that the connectors (on the center airbag sensor side and occupant classification ECU side) are not damaged. OK: The connectors are not deformed or damaged. NG --  REPLACE HARNESS AND CONNECTOR OK -- Continue to next step.

CHECK OCCUPANT CLASSIFICATION SYSTEM CIRCUIT (a) Using a service wire, connect terminals 16 (FSR+) and 22 (FSR-) of connector B. NOTICE: Do not forcibly insert a service wire into the terminals of the connector when connecting the wire. (b) Measure the resistance according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard resistance: (c) Disconnect the service wire from connector B. (d) Measure the resistance according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard resistance: (e) Connect the negative (-) terminal cable to the battery, and wait for at least 2 seconds. (f) Turn the engine switch on (IG). (g) Measure the voltage according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard voltage: NG --  CHECK FLOOR WIRE OK --  REPLACE CENTER AIRBAG SENSOR ASSEMBLY  See: Relays and Modules/Relays and Modules - Restraints and Safety Systems/Air Bag Control Module/Service and Repair/Components

CHECK FLOOR WIRE (a) Turn the engine switch off. (b) Disconnect the negative (-) terminal cable from the battery, and wait for at least 90 seconds. (c) Disconnect the front seat wire RH connector from the floor wire. (d) Using a service wire, connect terminals 16 (FSR+) and 22 (FSR-) of connector B. (e) Measure the resistance according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard resistance: (f) Disconnect the service wire from connector B. (g) Measure the resistance according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard resistance: (h) Turn the engine switch off. (i) Disconnect the negative (-) terminal cable from the battery, and wait for at least 90 seconds. (j) Disconnect the front seat wire RH connector from the floor wire. (k) Connect the negative (-) terminal cable to the battery, and wait for at least 2 seconds. (l) Turn the engine switch on (IG). (m)Measure the voltage according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard voltage: NG --  REPLACE FLOOR WIRE OK --  REPLACE FRONT SEAT WIRE RH

dtcs.description: 35 is not output. HINT Codes other than DTC B1653/35 may be output at this time, but they are not related to this check. steps.instruction: CHECK PASSENGER AIRBAG ON/OFF INDICATOR CONDITION (a) Turn the engine switch on (IG). (b) Check the passenger airbag ON/OFF indicator operation. HINT Refer to the normal condition of the passenger airbag ON/OFF indicator    See: Restraints and Safety Systems/Air Bag Systems/Testing and Inspection/Initial Inspection and Diagnostic Overview/Diagnosis System. Result B --  CHECK CONNECTION OF CONNECTORS A -- Continue to next step.

CHECK CONNECTION OF CONNECTORS (a) Turn the engine switch off. (b) Disconnect the negative (-) terminal cable from the battery, and wait for at least 90 seconds. (c) w/ Multi-display: Check that the connectors are properly connected to the center airbag sensor and the display and navigation module display. OK: The connectors are properly connected. (d) w/o Multi-display: Check that the connectors are properly connected to the center airbag sensor and the integration control and panel. OK: The connectors are properly connected. NG --  CONNECT CONNECTORS PROPERLY OK -- Continue to next step.

CHECK CONNECTORS (a) w/ Multi-display: (1) Disconnect the connectors from the center airbag sensor and the display and navigation module display. (2) Check that the connectors (on the center airbag sensor side and display and navigation module display side) are not damaged. OK: The connectors are not deformed or damaged. (b) w/o Multi-display: (1) Disconnect the connectors from the center airbag sensor and the integration control and panel. (2) Check that the connectors (on the center airbag sensor side and integration control and panel side) are not damaged. OK: The connectors are not deformed or damaged. NG --  REPLACE INSTRUMENT PANEL WIRE OK -- Continue to next step.

CHECK PASSENGER AIRBAG ON/OFF INDICATOR (a) Connect the connector to the display and navigation module display or integration control and panel. (b) Connect the negative (-) terminal cable to the battery, and wait for at least 2 seconds. (c) Turn the engine switch on (IG). (d) Check the passenger airbag ON/OFF indicator operation. OK: The passenger airbag ON/OFF indicator does not come on. NG --  CHECK INSTRUMENT PANEL WIRE (CENTER AIRBAG SENSOR - PASSENGER AIRBAG ON/OFF INDICATOR) OK -- Continue to next step.

CHECK CENTER AIRBAG SENSOR (a) Connect the connector to the center airbag sensor. (b) Connect the negative (-) terminal cable to the battery, and wait for at least 2 seconds. (c) Turn the engine switch on (IG), and wait for at least 60 seconds. (d) Clear the DTCs    See: Restraints and Safety Systems/Air Bag Systems/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes. (e) Turn the engine switch off. (f) Turn the engine switch on (IG), and wait for at least 60 seconds. (g) Check for DTCs    See: Restraints and Safety Systems/Air Bag Systems/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes. OK:

CHECK INSTRUMENT PANEL WIRE (CENTER AIRBAG SENSOR - PASSENGER AIRBAG ON/OFF INDICATOR) (a) Turn the engine switch off. (b) Disconnect the negative (-) terminal cable from the battery, and wait for at least 90 seconds. (c) w/ Multi-display: Disconnect the connector from the display and navigation module display. (d) w/o Multi-display: Disconnect the connector from the integration control and panel. (e) Using a service wire, connect terminals 23 (PAON) and 17 (P-AB) of connector B. NOTICE: Do not forcibly insert a service wire into the terminals of the connector when connecting the wire. (f) Measure the resistance according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard resistance: w/ Multi-display w/o Multi-display (g) Disconnect the service wire from connector B. (h) Measure the resistance according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard resistance: w/ Multi-display w/o Multi-display (i) Connect the negative (-) terminal cable to the battery, and wait for at least 2 seconds. (j) Turn the engine switch on (IG). (k) Measure the voltage according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard voltage: w/ Multi-display w/o Multi-display Result A --  REPLACE DISPLAY AND NAVIGATION MODULE DISPLAY  See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Entertainment Systems/Television / Monitor/Service and Repair/Removal and Replacement/Multi-Display (w/o Navigation System)/Components B --  REPLACE DISPLAY AND NAVIGATION MODULE DISPLAY  See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Navigation System/Service and Repair/Removal and Replacement/Display and Navigation Assembly/Removal C --  REPLACE DISPLAY AND NAVIGATION MODULE DISPLAY  See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Navigation System/Navigation Display/Service and Repair/Removal and Replacement/Multi-Display (w/ Navigation System)/Removal D --  REPLACE INTEGRATION CONTROL AND PANEL ASSEMBLY  See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Entertainment Systems/Television / Monitor/Service and Repair/Removal and Replacement/Multi-Display (w/o Navigation System)/Components E --  REPLACE INSTRUMENT PANEL WIRE

(c) w/ Multi-display: Check that the connectors are properly connected to the center airbag sensor and the display and navigation module display. OK: The connectors are properly connected. (d) w/o Multi-display: Check that the connectors are properly connected to the center airbag sensor and the integration control and panel. OK: The connectors are properly connected. NG --  CONNECT CONNECTORS PROPERLY OK -- Continue to next step.

CHECK CONNECTORS (a) w/ Multi-display: (1) Disconnect the connectors from the center airbag sensor and the display and navigation module display. (2) Check that the connectors (on the center airbag sensor side and display and navigation module display side) are not damaged. OK: The connectors are not deformed or damaged. (b) w/o Multi-display: (1) Disconnect the connectors from the center airbag sensor and the integration control and panel. (2) Check that the connectors (on the center airbag sensor side and integration control and panel side) are not damaged. OK: The connectors are not deformed or damaged. NG --  REPLACE INSTRUMENT PANEL WIRE OK -- Continue to next step.

(j) Turn the engine switch off. (k) Turn the engine switch on (IG). (l) Clear the DTCs stored in the memory    See: Restraints and Safety Systems/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes. (d) Turn the engine switch off. (e) Turn the engine switch on (IG). (f) Using the Techstream, check for the DTCs of the occupant classification ECU    See: Restraints and Safety Systems/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes. OK:

REPLACE CENTER AIRBAG SENSOR ASSEMBLY (a) Turn the engine switch off. (b) Disconnect the negative (-) terminal cable from the battery, and wait for at least 90 seconds. (c) Replace the center airbag sensor    See: Relays and Modules/Relays and Modules - Restraints and Safety Systems/Air Bag Control Module/Service and Repair/Removal. NEXT -- Continue to next step.
PERFORM SENSITIVITY CHECK (a) Perform the sensitivity check    See: Restraints and Safety Systems/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning/Initialization. NEXT --  END

CHECK FLOOR WIRE (a) Turn the engine switch off. (b) Disconnect the negative (-) terminal cable from the battery, and wait for at least 90 seconds. (c) Disconnect the front seat wire RH connector from the floor wire. (d) Connect the negative (-) terminal cable to the battery, and wait for at least 2 seconds. (e) Turn the engine switch on (IG). (f) Measure the voltage according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard voltage: (g) Disconnect the front seat wire RH connector from the floor wire. (h) Disconnect the negative (-) terminal cable from the battery. (i) Using service wire, connect terminals 16 (FSR+) and 22 (FSR-) of connector B. NOTICE: Do not forcibly insert a service wire into the terminals of the connector when connecting the wire. (j) Measure the resistance according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard resistance: (k) Disconnect the service wire from connector B. (l) Measure the resistance according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard resistance: NG --  REPLACE FLOOR WIRE OK --  REPLACE FRONT SEAT WIRE RH

dtcs.description: is recorded when a malfunction is detected in the occupant classification ECU. HINT When DTC B1650/32 is detected as a result of troubleshooting for the "AIRBAG SYSTEM", check the DTCs stored in the occupant classification ECU. When DTC B1795 is output, perform troubleshooting for the DTC. NOTICE: * w/ Navigation System (for HDD): After the engine switch is turned off, the display and navigation module display (HDD navigation system) records various types of memory and settings. As a result, after turning the engine switch off, be sure to wait for the time specified in the following table before disconnecting the cable from the negative (-) battery terminal. Waiting Time before Disconnecting Cable from Negative (-) Battery Terminal: * When disconnecting the cable, some systems need to be initialized after the cable is reconnected See: Testing and Inspection/Repair Instruction - Initialization. HINT * If troubleshooting (wire harness inspection) is difficult to perform, remove the front passenger seat installation bolts to see the undersurface of the seat cushion. * In the above case, hold the seat so that it does not fall down. Holding the seat for a long period of time may cause a problem, such as seat rail deformation. Hold the seat only as necessary. PROCEDURE

steps.instruction: CHECK FOR DTC (a) Turn the engine switch on (IG). (b) Clear the DTCs stored in the occupant classification ECU    See: Restraints and Safety Systems/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes. (c) Clear the DTCs stored in the center airbag sensor    See: Restraints and Safety Systems/Air Bag Systems/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes. (d) Turn the engine switch off. (e) Turn the engine switch on (IG), and wait for at least 10 seconds. (f) Check for the DTCs    See: Restraints and Safety Systems/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes. OK:

REPLACE FRONT SEAT SET (OCCUPANT CLASSIFICATION ECU) (a) Connect the Techstream to the DLC3. (b) Turn the engine switch on (IG). (c) Using the Techstream, save the ECU data from the ECU which was installed on the vehicle    See: Restraints and Safety Systems/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning/Registration. (d) Turn the engine switch off. (e) Disconnect the negative (-) terminal cable from the battery, and wait for at least 90 seconds. (f) Replace the front seat set (occupant classification ECU)    See: Body and Frame/Seats/Service and Repair/Overhaul/Front Seat Assembly/Disassembly. (g) Connect the negative (-) terminal cable to the battery. (h) Turn the engine switch on (IG). (i) Using the Techstream, load the previous ECU data stored in the Techstream into the new ECU    See: Restraints and Safety Systems/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning/Registration. (j) Turn the engine switch off. (k) Turn the engine switch on (IG). (l) Clear the DTCs stored in the memory    See: Restraints and Safety Systems/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes. HINT If DTCs are not cleared at this time, past DTCs will remain. NEXT -- Continue to next step.
PERFORM SENSITIVITY CHECK (a) Perform the sensitivity check    See: Restraints and Safety Systems/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning/Initialization. NEXT --  END

dtcs.description: 51-B1803/51: Short in Driver Side Squib Circuit

51 - Short to B+ in Driver Side Squib Circuit * The driver side squib circuit consists of the center airbag sensor, the spiral cable, and the steering pad. * The circuit instructs the SRS to deploy when deployment conditions are met. * These DTCs are recorded when a malfunction is detected in the driver side squib circuit. NOTICE: After the engine switch is turned off, the HDD navigation system requires approximately 6 minutes to record various types of memory and settings. As a result, after turning the engine switch off, wait 6 minutes or more before disconnecting the cable from the negative (-) battery terminal. HINT * Perform the simulation method by selecting "check mode" (signal check) with the Techstream See: Restraints and Safety Systems/Air Bag Systems/Testing and Inspection/Scan Tool Testing and Procedures/Check Mode Procedure. * After selecting "check mode" (signal check), perform the simulation method by wiggling each connector of the airbag system or driving the vehicle on various types of roads See: Restraints and Safety Systems/Air Bag Systems/Testing and Inspection/Initial Inspection and Diagnostic Overview/Diagnosis System. PROCEDURE

steps.instruction: CHECK STEERING PAD (DRIVER SIDE SQUIB) (a) Turn the engine switch off. (b) Disconnect the negative (-) terminal cable from the battery, and wait for at least 90 seconds. (c) Disconnect the connectors from the steering pad. (d) Connect the white wire side of SST (resistance: 2.1 Ohms) to connector E (orange connector).

CHECK CONNECTOR (a) Turn the engine switch off. (b) Disconnect the negative (-) terminal cable from the battery, and wait for at least 90 seconds. (c) Disconnect SST from connector E. (d) Check that the spiral cable connectors (on the steering pad side) are not damaged. OK: The lock button is not disengaged, or the claw of the lock is not deformed or damaged. NG --  REPLACE SPIRAL CABLE  See: Restraints and Safety Systems/Air Bag Systems/Clockspring Assembly / Spiral Cable/Service and Repair/Components OK -- Continue to next step.

CHECK DRIVER SIDE SQUIB CIRCUIT (a) Disconnect the connectors from the center airbag sensor. (b) Connect the negative (-) terminal cable to the battery, and wait for at least 2 seconds. (f) Disconnect the negative (-) terminal cable from the battery, and wait for at least 90 seconds. (g) Measure the resistance according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard resistance: (h) Release the activation prevention mechanism built into connector B    See: Restraints and Safety Systems/Air Bag Systems/Testing and Inspection/Initial Inspection and Diagnostic Overview/Diagnosis System. (c) Turn the engine switch on (IG), and wait for at least 60 seconds. (d) Clear the DTCs    See: Restraints and Safety Systems/Air Bag Systems/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes. (e) Turn the engine switch off. (f) Turn the engine switch on (IG), and wait for at least 60 seconds. (g) Check for DTCs    See: Restraints and Safety Systems/Air Bag Systems/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes. OK: DTC B1800, B1801, B1802, B1803 or 51 is not output. HINT Codes other than DTC B1800, B1801, B1802, B1803 and 51 may be output at this time, but they are not related to this check. NG --  REPLACE CENTER AIRBAG SENSOR ASSEMBLY  See: Relays and Modules/Relays and Modules - Restraints and Safety Systems/Air Bag Control Module/Service and Repair/Components OK --  USE SIMULATION METHOD TO CHECK  See: Restraints and Safety Systems/Air Bag Systems/Testing and Inspection/Initial Inspection and Diagnostic Overview/Diagnosis System

dtcs.description: Short to B+ in Seat Belt Motor RH Circuit

Short to B+ in Seat Belt Motor LH Circuit The seat belt control ECU receives collision information from the driving support ECU and skid control ECU via CAN communication, and tightens the seat belt by operating the motor inside the front seat outer belt assembly. When DTC B2000 is detected, DTCs B2016, B2026, B2027, and B2035 will not be detected. When DTC B2005 is detected, DTCs B2021, B2031, B2032, and B2035 will not be detected.

steps.instruction: CHECK FOR DTC (a) Enter the following menus: Body Electrical / Pre-Collision 1 / Trouble Code / (b) Select "Clear" and clear the data. (c) Recheck for DTCs. OK:

dtcs.description: Power Supply Relay Malfunction Open If the seat belt control ECU judges that there is a malfunction in its internal circuit as a result of self-diagnosis, or if the power source voltage (+B) is not input to the seat belt control ECU, this DTC will be stored.

steps.instruction: INSPECT SEAT BELT CONTROL ECU (+B TERMINAL VOLTAGE) (a) Disconnect the negative (-) battery terminal.

dtcs.description: ABS Malfunction This DTC is output when a malfunction in the brake system is detected. This circuit consists of the skid control ECU and seat belt control ECU. Signals are sent from the skid control ECU to the seal belt control ECU through the CAN communication line. When DTC B2060 is detected, ABS system DTCs will also be detected. PROCEDURE

steps.instruction: CHECK FOR DTC (ELECTRONICALLY CONTROLLED BRAKE SYSTEM) (a) Enter the following menus: Chassis / ABS/VSC/TRAC / Trouble Code / (b) Check for DTCs. Result B --  GO TO ELECTRONICALLY CONTROLLED BRAKE SYSTEM  See: Diagnostic Trouble Code Descriptions/Electronically Controlled Brake System A --  REPLACE SEAT BELT CONTROL ECU  See: Relays and Modules/Relays and Modules - Restraints and Safety Systems/Seat Belt Control Module/Service and Repair/Removal Pre-Collision System (w/ Driver Monitor Camera)

dtcs.description: Vehicle Type Signal Malfunction The seat belt control ECU is connected to the main body ECU through CAN communication. The seat belt control ECU receives vehicle model information from the main body ECU and determines whether the seat belt buckle switch information from the airbag sensor center is for the driver seat or the front passenger seat.

steps.instruction: CHECK CAN COMMUNICATION SYSTEM (a) Select "Bus Check" from the "System Select"    See: Powertrain Management/Computers and Control Systems/Information Bus/Testing and Inspection/Initial Inspection and Diagnostic Overview/CAN Communication System/Diagnosis System (b) Select "Communication Malfunction DTC" from the "Bus Check" screen, and then select "Enter"    See: Powertrain Management/Computers and Control Systems/Information Bus/Testing and Inspection/Initial Inspection and Diagnostic Overview/CAN Communication System/Diagnosis System Result B --  GO TO CAN COMMUNICATION SYSTEM  See: Diagnostic Trouble Code Descriptions/CAN Communication System A -- Continue to next step.
CHECK MAIN BODY ECU (a) Enter the following menus: Body Electrical / Pre-Collision 1 / Trouble Code / (b) Select "Clear" and clear the data. (c) Recheck for DTCs. OK:
REPLACE MAIN BODY ECU (a) Replace the main body ECU. NEXT -- Continue to next step.
CHECK SEAT BELT CONTROL ECU (a) Using the Techstream, enter the following menus: Body Electrical / Pre-Collision 1 / Trouble Code / (b) Select "CLEAR" and clear the data. (c) Recheck for DTCs. OK:

dtcs.description: Driver Side Seat Belt Buckle Switch Circuit Malfunction

Passenger Side Seat Belt Buckle Switch Circuit Malfunction The seat belt control ECU receives driver side buckle switch condition information from the center airbag sensor through the CAN communication line and controls the pre-collision system. This code is output when a buckle switch malfunction signal is received from the airbag system. When DTC B2074 is detected, DTC B1655 is also output. When DTC B2075 is detected, DTC B1650 is also output. HINT * If DTC B1655 is not cleared first, DTC B2074 continues to be output. * If DTC B1650 is not cleared first, DTC B2075 continues to be output. PROCEDURE

steps.instruction: CHECK AIRBAG SYSTEM (a) Enter the following menus: Body Electrical / SRS Airbag / Trouble Code / (b) Check for DTC. Result B --  GO TO AIRBAG SYSTEM  See: Diagnostic Trouble Code Descriptions/Airbag System A --  REPLACE SEAT BELT CONTROL ECU  See: Relays and Modules/Relays and Modules - Restraints and Safety Systems/Seat Belt Control Module/Service and Repair/Removal Pre-Collision System (w/ Driver Monitor Camera)

dtcs.description: Driving Support System Malfunction The seat belt control ECU receives collision information from the driving support ECU and skid control ECU via CAN communication, and tightens the seat belt by operating the motor inside the front seat outer belt assembly. When the driving support ECU detects a malfunction in the dynamic radar cruise control system, and pre-collision system ("Pre-Collision 2*2" part), or detects that the object recognition camera sensor has not been adjusted, the malfunction information is sent to the seat belt control ECU, which outputs this DTC. When DTC B2090 is detected, other DTCs output by the driving support ECU (dynamic radar cruise control system, Pre-Collision 2) are also output. HINT If the object recognition camera sensor is replaced with a new one and the adjustment of the object recognition camera sensor has not been performed, DTC B2090 is output. HINT When clearing the DTCs, first clear the DTC output from the driving support ECU, and then clear the DTC output from the seat belt control ECU. PROCEDURE

steps.instruction: CHECK DRIVING SUPPORT ECU (a) Enter the following menus: Body Electrical / Pre-Collision 2, Cruise Control / Trouble Code. (b) Check for DTCs. Result B --  GO TO RELEVANT FLOWCHART A -- Continue to next step.
CHECK SEAT BELT CONTROL ECU (a) Enter the following menus: Body Electrical / Pre-Collision 1 / Trouble Code / (b) Select "Clear" and clear the data. (c) Recheck for DTCs. OK:
CHECK SEAT BELT CONTROL ECU (a) Enter the following menus: Body Electrical / Pre-Collision 1 / Trouble Code. (b) Select "Clear" and clear the data. (c) Recheck for DTC. OK:

dtcs.description: 1UR-FSE STARTING: SMART ACCESS SYSTEM WITH PUSH-BUTTON START: B2282, B2283: Vehicle Speed Signal Malfunction

Vehicle Speed Sensor Malfunction The main body ECU and the combination meter are connected with a cable and the CAN communication line. DTC B2282 is output when signals, transmitted through the cable and the communication line, do not match. The skid control ECU converts these signals into 4-pulse signals and sends them to the combination meter. After these signals are converted into a more precise rectangular waveform by the waveform shaping circuit inside the combination meter, they are then transmitted to the main body ECU. The main body ECU determines the vehicle speed based on the frequencies of these pulse signals. PROCEDURE

steps.instruction: READ VALUE USING TECHSTREAM (VEHICLE SPEED SIGNAL) (a) Use the Data List to check if the vehicle speed signal is functioning properly. Main Body OK: Stop appears on screen. NG --  REPLACE MAIN BODY ECU OK -- Continue to next step.

(c) Measure the resistance according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard resistance: NG --  REPAIR OR REPLACE HARNESS OR CONNECTOR OK -- Continue to next step.

CHECK MAIN BODY ECU (OPERATION) (a) Temporarily replace the main body ECU with a new or normally functioning one. (b) Check that the engine starts normally. OK: Engine starts normally. NG --  GO TO COMBINATION METER SYSTEM  See: Instrument Panel, Gauges and Warning Indicators/Testing and Inspection/Initial Inspection and Diagnostic Overview/How To Proceed With Troubleshooting OK --  END (MAIN BODY ECU IS FAULTY)

dtcs.description: Power Window Motor Malfunction The power window regulator motor assembly is driven by operating the power window switch. The power window regulator motor assembly consists of the motor, regulator and ECU. This DTC is output when the power window regulator motor has a malfunction, or the ECU built into the power window regulator motor determines that the fully closed window position has deviated approximately 20 mm (0.79 in.) or more from the normal position. HINT This DTC may occur for all the windows. NOTICE: * When the power window regulator motor is reinstalled or replaced, the power window control system must be initialized. * After a door glass or a door glass run has been replaced, the jam protection function may operate unexpectedly when the AUTO UP function is used. In such cases, the AUTO UP function can be resumed by repeating the following operation at least 5 times: (1) Close the power window by fully pulling up the power window switch and holding it in the AUTO UP position. (2) Open the power window by fully pushing down the switch. Front power window regulator motor LH: Front power window regulator motor RH: Rear power window regulator motor LH: Rear power window regulator motor RH: PROCEDURE

steps.instruction: PERFORM ACTIVE TEST USING TECHSTREAM (POWER WINDOW) (a) Select the Active Test, use the Techstream to generate a control command, and then check that the power window regulator motor UP/DOWN.

(b) Check that the power window operates normally by opening and closing the window. OK: Power window operates normally. Result: B --  REPLACE FRONT POWER WINDOW REGULATOR MOTOR ASSEMBLY LH  See: Body and Frame/Doors, Hood and Trunk/Doors/Front Door/Front Door Window Motor/Service and Repair/Removal C --  REPLACE FRONT POWER WINDOW REGULATOR MOTOR ASSEMBLY RH  See: Body and Frame/Doors, Hood and Trunk/Doors/Front Door/Front Door Window Motor/Service and Repair/Removal D --  REPLACE REAR POWER WINDOW REGULATOR MOTOR ASSEMBLY LH  See: Body and Frame/Doors, Hood and Trunk/Doors/Rear Door/Rear Door Window Motor/Service and Repair/Removal E --  REPLACE REAR POWER WINDOW REGULATOR MOTOR ASSEMBLY RH  See: Body and Frame/Doors, Hood and Trunk/Doors/Rear Door/Rear Door Window Motor/Service and Repair/Removal A -- Continue to next step.

CHECK WHETHER PARTS HAVE BEEN INSTALLED CORRECTLY (a) Check that the power window components are installed correctly. OK: Power window components are installed correctly. HINT When the power window components are installed correctly, the problem has been caused by battery disconnection while the engine switch is on (IG) or the motor being overheated. NG --  INSTALL PARTS CORRECTLY OK --  END

dtcs.description: Power Window Switch Malfunction The power window regulator motor assembly consists of the motor, regulator and ECU. The power window regulator motor assembly is driven by operating the power window regulator switch. This DTC is output when the ECU built into the regulator motor assembly determines that the power window switch is stuck. HINT This DTC may occur for all the windows. NOTICE: * When the power window regulator motor is reinstalled or replaced, the power window control system must be initialized. * After a door glass or a door glass run has been replaced, the jam protection function may operate unexpectedly when the AUTO UP function is used. In such cases, the AUTO UP function can be resumed by repeating the following operation at least 5 times: (1) Close the power window by fully pulling up the power window switch and holding it in the AUTO UP position. (2) Open the power window by fully pushing down the switch. Multiplex network master switch: Front power window regulator motor LH: Front power window regulator motor RH: Rear power window regulator motor LH: Rear power window regulator motor RH: PROCEDURE

dtcs.description: Glass Position Initialization Incomplete The power window regulator motor assembly is driven by operating the power window switch. The power window regulator motor assembly has motor, regulator and ECU functions. When the ECU determines that the power window regulator motor has not been initialized, DTC B2313 is set. HINT This DTC may occur for all the windows. NOTICE: * When the power window regulator motor is reinstalled or replaced, the power window control system must be initialized. * After a door glass or a door glass run has been replaced, the jam protection function may operate unexpectedly when the AUTO UP function is used. In such cases, the AUTO UP function can be resumed by repeating the following operation at least 5 times: (1) Close the power window by fully pulling up the power window switch and holding it in the AUTO UP position. (2) Open the power window by fully pushing down the switch. Front power window regulator motor LH: Front power window regulator motor RH: Rear power window regulator motor LH: Rear power window regulator motor RH: PROCEDURE

steps.instruction: CHECK FOR DTC (a) Turn the engine switch on (IG) and initialize the power window regulator motor    See: Windows and Glass/Windows/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning/Initialization. (b) Turn the engine switch off. (c) Wait for at least 10 seconds, and then turn the engine switch on (IG). (d) Check for DTCs. Result: B --  REPLACE FRONT POWER WINDOW REGULATOR MOTOR ASSEMBLY LH  See: Body and Frame/Doors, Hood and Trunk/Doors/Front Door/Front Door Window Motor/Service and Repair/Removal C --  REPLACE FRONT POWER WINDOW REGULATOR MOTOR ASSEMBLY RH  See: Body and Frame/Doors, Hood and Trunk/Doors/Front Door/Front Door Window Motor/Service and Repair/Removal D --  REPLACE REAR POWER WINDOW REGULATOR MOTOR ASSEMBLY LH  See: Body and Frame/Doors, Hood and Trunk/Doors/Rear Door/Rear Door Window Motor/Service and Repair/Removal E --  REPLACE REAR POWER WINDOW REGULATOR MOTOR ASSEMBLY RH  See: Body and Frame/Doors, Hood and Trunk/Doors/Rear Door/Rear Door Window Motor/Service and Repair/Removal A --  END (POWER WINDOW CONTROL SYSTEM HAS NOT BEEN INITIALIZED)

dtcs.description: Transistor Relay Overload Malfunction The electric current flowing in the transistor inside the rear junction block is examined by the rear junction block ECU. When excessive current is detected, DTC B2402 is output. Turning off the engine switch clears the DTC. PROCEDURE

dtcs.description: CAN Communication Malfunction * The AFS ECU receives an engine running states signal and shift position signal from the ECM using CAN communication. * The AFS ECU receives front speed sensor LH and RH signals from the skid control ECU using CAN communication. * The AFS ECU receives the headlight states signal from the main body ECU using CAN communication. * The AFS ECU sends an AFS OFF indicator light ON or OFF demand signal to the combination meter ECU using CAN communication. PROCEDURE

steps.instruction: CLEAR DTC (a) Clear the DTC    See: Lighting and Horns/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes. NEXT -- Continue to next step.
CHECK DTC (a) Check for DTC    See: Lighting and Horns/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes. OK:

CHECK DTC (CAN COMMUNICATION SYSTEM) (a) Check for DTC    See: Powertrain Management/Computers and Control Systems/Information Bus/Testing and Inspection/Initial Inspection and Diagnostic Overview/CAN Communication System/Diagnosis System. OK: CAN communication system DTC output does not occur. NG --  GO TO CAN COMMUNICATION SYSTEM  See: Powertrain Management/Computers and Control Systems/Information Bus/Service Precautions/CAN Communication System OK --  REPLACE AFS ECU  See: Lighting and Horns/Headlamp/Headlamp Alignment Control Module/Service and Repair/Removal

dtcs.description: Steering Wheel Position Undecided The AFS ECU receives steering wheel position information (turned to right or left) from the main body ECU via CAN communication. NOTICE: First perform the communication function inspections in HOW TO PROCEED WITH TROUBLESHOOTING to confirm that there are no CAN communication malfunctions before troubleshooting this symptom. PROCEDURE

dtcs.description: Horn Circuit Transistor This DTC is output when one of the following occurs: 1) vehicle horn (high pitched or low pitched) is malfunctioning; 2) front light ECU (front controller) is malfunctioning; or 3) the wire harness between the front light ECU and vehicle horn is open or shorted. PROCEDURE

steps.instruction: CHECK HORN ASSEMBLY (OPERATION) (a) Check if the horns sound normally. Result HINT If both horns do not sound, there may be a malfunction in the front light ECU. B --  REPLACE FRONT LIGHT ECU (FRONT CONTROLLER) A -- Continue to next step.

PERFORM ACTIVE TEST USING TECHSTREAM (VEHICLE HORNS) (a) Operate the Techstream according to the steps on the display and select "Active Test". Body No.5 OK: Horn operates normally. NG --  INSPECT HORN ASSEMBLY OK --  REPLACE FRONT LIGHT ECU (FRONT CONTROLLER)

INSPECT HORN ASSEMBLY (a) Remove the horn for high pitched horn    See: Lighting and Horns/Horn/Service and Repair/High Pitched Horn/Removal or low pitched horn    See: Lighting and Horns/Horn/Service and Repair/Low Pitched Horn/Removal. (b) Check operation of the horn. OK: Result B --  REPLACE HIGH PITCHED HORN ASSEMBLY  See: Lighting and Horns/Horn/Service and Repair/High Pitched Horn/Removal C --  REPLACE LOW PITCHED HORN ASSEMBLY  See: Lighting and Horns/Horn/Service and Repair/Low Pitched Horn/Removal A -- Continue to next step.

CHECK HARNESS AND CONNECTOR (FRONT LIGHT ECU - HORN) (a) Disconnect the B14 ECU connector. (b) Disconnect the B6*1 or B10*2 horn connector. *1: for High Pitched Horn *2: for Low Pitched Horn (c) Measure the resistance according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard resistance: for High pitched horn for Low pitched horn NG --  REPAIR OR REPLACE HARNESS OR CONNECTOR OK --  REPLACE FRONT LIGHT ECU (FRONT CONTROLLER)

(c) Measure the resistance according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard resistance: NG --  REPAIR OR REPLACE HARNESS OR CONNECTOR OK --  REPLACE FRONT LIGHT ECU

Key Code Confirm Signal Malfunction A key code confirmation signal (ON or OFF) is sent from the main body ECU to the multiplex tilt and telescopic ECU via the communication bus. The multiplex tilt and telescopic ECU uses this signal to operate the auto away/return function. PROCEDURE

steps.instruction: CHECK OTHER DTC OUTPUT HINT The power tilt and power telescopic steering column system uses the CAN communication system, so check its diagnostic system before proceeding to troubleshooting. (a) Check if DTC B2621 is output. Result: HINT * If DTC B2621 besides B2602 and B2606 are output, perform troubleshooting for B2621 first. *

dtcs.description: Communication Interruption The multiplex tilt and telescopic ECU forms a network with the ECUs of other systems through the communication bus. Each ECU informs the other ECUs that they are connected to the network by outputting a specified signal (periodical signal) to the communication bus at a regular schedule. The multiplex tilt and telescopic ECU detects the connection of each ECU based on the signal. PROCEDURE

steps.instruction: CHECK DTC (CAN COMMUNICATION SYSTEM) (a) Check the DTC of the CAN communication system    See: Powertrain Management/Computers and Control Systems/Information Bus/Service Precautions/CAN Communication System. Result: B --  GO TO CAN COMMUNICATION SYSTEM  See: Powertrain Management/Computers and Control Systems/Information Bus/Service Precautions/CAN Communication System A -- Continue to next step.

Tilt & Telescopic: OK: "ON" is displayed. NG --  CHECK SMART ACCESS SYSTEM WITH PUSH-BUTTON START  See: Starting and Charging/Starting System/Testing and Inspection/Initial Inspection and Diagnostic Overview OK -- Continue to next step.

dtcs.description: Tilt and Telescopic Manual Switch Circuit Malfunction Different voltage values are input to the multiplex tilt and telescopic ECU by operating the manual switch. The multiplex tilt and telescopic ECU then judges which motor and in which direction that the motor should rotate based on the voltage value. PROCEDURE

Tilt & Telescopic: Result: A --  REPLACE MULTIPLEX TILT AND TELESCOPIC ECU  See: Relays and Modules/Relays and Modules - Steering and Suspension/Relays and Modules - Steering/Steering Column Control Module/Service and Repair/Removal B -- Continue to next step.

(b) Disconnect the L25 connector from the windshield wiper switch. (c) Measure the resistance according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard resistance: NG --  REPAIR OR REPLACE HARNESS OR CONNECTOR OK -- Continue to next step.

READ VALUE USING TECHSTREAM (TILT AND TELESCOPIC MANUAL SWITCH) (a) Use the Data List to check if the tilt and telescopic manual switch is functioning properly. dtcs.description: Tilt Position Sensor or Tilt Motor Circuit Malfunction The tilt motor is operated by the power source voltage supplied from the multiplex tilt and telescopic ECU and tilts the steering column up and down. The tilt position sensor (hall IC) in the tilt motor detects the tilt angle of the steering column and outputs a signal to the CPU based on that tilt. HINT Limit positions can be confirmed on the screen of the Techstream. PROCEDURE

steps.instruction: PERFORM ACTIVE TEST USING TECHSTREAM (TILT UP/DOWN) (a) Select the Active Test, use the Techstream to generate a control command, and then check that the tilt up/down. Tilt & Telescopic: OK: Steering wheel tilts up (down). OK --  CHECK HARNESS AND CONNECTOR (MULTIPLEX TILT AND TELESCOPIC ECU - TILT POSITION SENSOR) NG -- Continue to next step.

CHECK HARNESS AND CONNECTOR (MULTIPLEX TILT AND TELESCOPIC ECU - TILT MOTOR) (a) Disconnect the L34 connector from the multiplex tilt and telescopic ECU. (b) Disconnect the L26 connector from the tilt motor. (c) Measure the resistance according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard resistance: NG --  REPAIR OR REPLACE HARNESS OR CONNECTOR OK -- Continue to next step.

INSPECT TILT MOTOR (a) Connect the positive (+) battery terminal to terminal 1 (TIM+) and the negative (-) battery terminal to terminal 2 (TIM-) of the tilt motor connector. Then confirm that the steering wheel tilts up. OK: The steering wheel tilts up. (b) Connect the negative (-) battery terminal to terminal 1 (TIM+) and the positive (+) battery terminal to terminal 2 (TIM-) of the tilt motor connector. Then confirm that the steering wheel tilts down. OK: The steering wheel tilts down. NG --  REPLACE STEERING COLUMN ASSEMBLY (TILT STEERING GEAR ASSEMBLY WITH MOTOR)  See: Steering and Suspension/Steering/Steering Column/Service and Repair/Removal and Replacement/Steering Column Assembly (w/ VGRS)/Removal OK --  REPLACE MULTIPLEX TILT AND TELESCOPIC ECU  See: Relays and Modules/Relays and Modules - Steering and Suspension/Relays and Modules - Steering/Steering Column Control Module/Service and Repair/Removal

(b) Disconnect the L26 connector from the tilt motor. (c) Measure the resistance according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard resistance: NG --  REPAIR OR REPLACE HARNESS OR CONNECTOR OK -- Continue to next step.

CHECK HARNESS AND CONNECTOR (MULTIPLEX TILT AND TELESCOPIC ECU - BODY GROUND) (a) Measure the resistance according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard resistance: NG --  REPAIR OR REPLACE HARNESS OR CONNECTOR OK -- Continue to next step.

CHECK TILT POSITION SENSOR (a) Reconnect the L26 connector to the tilt motor. (b) Measure the voltage according to the value(s) in the table below. dtcs.description: Telescopic Position Sensor or Telescopic Motor Circuit Malfunction The telescopic motor is operated by the power source voltage supplied from the multiplex tilt and telescopic ECU and slides the steering column forward and backward. The telescopic position sensor (hall IC) in the telescopic motor detects the sliding position in the forward and backward directions of the steering column and outputs a signal to the multiplex tilt and telescopic ECU based on that sliding amount. HINT Limit positions can be confirmed on the screen of the Techstream. HINT The wire harness between the multiplex tilt and telescopic ECU and telescopic motor is provided with the steering column assembly. PROCEDURE

steps.instruction: PERFORM ACTIVE TEST USING TECHSTREAM (TELESCO SHORT/LONG) (a) Select the Active Test, use the Techstream to generate a control command, and then check that the telescopic long /short. Tilt & Telescopic: OK: The steering column contracts (extends). OK --  CHECK MULTIPLEX TILT AND TELESCOPIC ECU (VCE TERMINAL VOLTAGE) NG -- Continue to next step.

CHECK TELESCOPIC MOTOR (a) Disconnect connector A from the multiplex tilt and telescopic ECU. (b) Connect the positive (+) battery terminal to terminal A-2 (TEM+) and the negative (-) battery terminal to terminal A-6 (TEM-) of the telescopic motor connector. Then confirm that the steering column contracts. OK: The steering column contracts. (c) Connect the negative (-) battery terminal to terminal A-2 (TEM+) and the positive (+) battery terminal to terminal A-6 (TEM-) of the telescopic motor connector. Then confirm that the steering column extends. OK: The steering column extends. NG --  REPLACE STEERING COLUMN ASSEMBLY (TILT STEERING GEAR ASSEMBLY WITH MOTOR)  See: Steering and Suspension/Steering/Steering Column/Service and Repair/Removal and Replacement/Steering Column Assembly (w/ VGRS)/Removal OK --  REPLACE MULTIPLEX TILT AND TELESCOPIC ECU  See: Relays and Modules/Relays and Modules - Steering and Suspension/Relays and Modules - Steering/Steering Column Control Module/Service and Repair/Removal

dtcs.description: Vehicles Speed Malfunction The multiplex tilt and telescopic ECU forms a network with the ECUs of other systems through the communication bus. Each ECU informs the other ECUs that they are connected to the network by outputting a specified signal (periodic signal) to the communication bus at a regular basis. The multiplex tilt and telescopic ECU receives vehicle speed signals from the combination meter via the communication bus to prevent improper operation of the auto away/return function during driving. HINT *: If the engine switch is turned off immediately after the vehicle comes to a sudden stop, the last vehicle speed signal may not be 0 km/h (0 mph). PROCEDURE

steps.instruction: CHECK CAN COMMUNICATION SYSTEM (a) Check the "COMMUNICATION STOP MODE TABLE" of the CAN communication system    See: Powertrain Management/Computers and Control Systems/Information Bus/Testing and Inspection/Initial Inspection and Diagnostic Overview/CAN Communication System/How to Proceed With Troubleshooting. Result: B --  GO TO CAN COMMUNICATION SYSTEM  See: Powertrain Management/Computers and Control Systems/Information Bus/Service Precautions/CAN Communication System A -- Continue to next step.

READ VALUE USING TECHSTREAM (SPEED SIGNAL) (a) Use the Data List to check if the speed signal is functioning properly. Tilt & Telescopic: Result: HINT Vehicle speed of 3 km/h (2 mph) or more may be displayed on the tester screen immediately after the vehicle comes to a sudden stop. This is caused by a delay in vehicle signal input via the communication bus and is not a malfunction. B --  REPLACE MULTIPLEX TILT AND TELESCOPIC ECU  See: Relays and Modules/Relays and Modules - Steering and Suspension/Relays and Modules - Steering/Steering Column Control Module/Service and Repair/Removal C --  CHECK COMBINATION METER SYSTEM  See: Instrument Panel, Gauges and Warning Indicators/Testing and Inspection/Initial Inspection and Diagnostic Overview/How To Proceed With Troubleshooting A --  REPLACE MULTIPLEX TILT AND TELESCOPIC ECU  See: Relays and Modules/Relays and Modules - Steering and Suspension/Relays and Modules - Steering/Steering Column Control Module/Service and Repair/Removal Front Power Seat Control System

dtcs.description: Open / Short in Steering Lock ECU The steering lock ECU and steering lock actuator are supplied as a unit. The steering lock ECU detects the steering lock position through the signals from the lock and unlock position sensors. The steering lock ECU activates the steering lock motor based on permission signals from the main body ECU and the certification ECU. The diagnosis information of the steering lock ECU is transmitted to the Techstream via the certification ECU as the steering lock ECU is not connected to the CAN communication system. HINT When the engine switch is off, the main body ECU may occasionally go into a non-active state called sleep mode. Therefore, before proceeding with the inspection, it is necessary to perform the following step to wake up the ECU: With the engine switch off, open the driver door. Then (with the engine switch still off) open and close any door several times at 1.5 second intervals. PROCEDURE

steps.instruction: CHECK DTC (CERTIFICATION ECU) (a) Check the DTCs    See: Steering and Suspension/Steering/Steering Column/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes/Steering Lock System. OK:

dtcs.description: Power Source Control ECU Malfunction The steering lock ECU activates the steering lock motor by the power from the main body ECU through the IGE circuit. This prevents the steering from being locked while the vehicle is moving. The diagnosis information of the steering lock ECU is transmitted to the Techstream via the certification ECU as the steering lock ECU is not connected to the CAN communication system. HINT When the engine switch is off, the main body ECU may occasionally go into a non-active state called sleep mode. Therefore, before proceeding with the inspection, it is necessary to perform the following step to wake up the ECU: With the engine switch off, open the driver door. Then (with the engine switch still off) open and close any door several times at 1.5 second intervals. PROCEDURE

dtcs.description: IG2 Signal Malfunction The steering lock ECU determines the on / off status of the engine switch through the IG2 signal circuit. After receiving an IG2 relay ON signal, the steering lock ECU determines that the vehicle is moving. The steering lock ECU does not lock the steering when it receives the IG2 relay ON signal. This prevents the steering from being locked while the vehicle is moving. The diagnosis information of the steering lock ECU is transmitted to the Techstream via the certification ECU as the steering lock ECU is not connected to the CAN communication system. HINT When the engine switch is off, the main body ECU may occasionally go into a non-active state called sleep mode. Therefore, before proceeding with the inspection, it is necessary to perform the following step to wake up the ECU: With the engine switch off, open the driver door. Then (with the engine switch still off) open and close any door several times at 1.5 second intervals. PROCEDURE
